## 7. Recording Process Details

### 7.1 ADB Screen Recording Command

```bash
# Start recording
adb shell screenrecord --output-format=h264 \
  --size=1280x720 \
  --bit-rate=4000000 \
  --time-limit=3600 \
  /sdcard/recording.mp4

# Monitor process
adb shell ps | grep screenrecord

# Stop recording (Ctrl+C or kill process)
adb shell kill <pid>

# Pull file from device
adb pull /sdcard/recording.mp4 ./recording.mp4

# Clean up
adb shell rm /sdcard/recording.mp4
```

### 7.2 Video Encoding Pipeline

```
Raw video from device
    │
    ▼
FFmpeg verification
    │
    ├─► Check format and codec
    │
    ├─► Verify no corruption
    │
    ▼
Generate thumbnail
    │
    ├─► Extract first frame
    │
    ├─► Resize to 320x180
    │
    ├─► Save as JPEG
    │
    ▼
Optional re-encoding
    │
    ├─► If resolution/fps/bitrate different from target
    │
    ├─► Re-encode using FFmpeg
    │
    ├─► Apply H.264 or H.265 codec
    │
    ▼
Final video ready
    │
    ├─► Calculate duration and file size
    │
    ├─► Verify playability
    │
    ▼
Upload to storage
```

### 7.3 FFmpeg Commands

```bash
# Verify video integrity
ffmpeg -v error -i input.mp4 -f null -

# Generate thumbnail
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 00:00:01 -vframes 1 -vf scale=320:180 thumbnail.jpg

# Re-encode video
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 \
  -c:v libx264 \
  -preset medium \
  -crf 23 \
  -s 1280x720 \
  -r 30 \
  -b:v 4000k \
  output.mp4

# Convert to WebM
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 \
  -c:v libvpx-vp9 \
  -crf 30 \
  -b:v 0 \
  output.webm

# Create HLS stream
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 \
  -c:v libx264 \
  -hls_time 10 \
  -hls_list_size 0 \
  output.m3u8
```

---

## 8. Storage Architecture

### 8.1 Local Storage (Development)

```
recordings/
├── 2026/
│   ├── 05/
│   │   ├── 16/
│   │   │   ├── device-uuid-1/
│   │   │   │   ├── recording-uuid-1.mp4
│   │   │   │   ├── recording-uuid-1.jpg
│   │   │   │   └── recording-uuid-1.json
```

### 8.2 S3 Storage (Production)

```
s3://audira-recordings/
├── 2026/
│   ├── 05/
│   │   ├── 16/
│   │   │   ├── device-uuid-1/
│   │   │   │   ├── recording-uuid-1.mp4
│   │   │   │   ├── recording-uuid-1.jpg
│   │   │   │   └── recording-uuid-1.json

# S3 Lifecycle Policy
- Transition to GLACIER after 90 days
- Delete after 180 days
- Enable versioning for recovery
```

### 8.3 Storage Optimization

```
# Compression
- H.264 codec: ~50MB per minute at 720p 30fps
- H.265 codec: ~25MB per minute at 720p 30fps (better compression)

# Deduplication
- Detect identical frames
- Store only unique frames
- Useful for static UI screens

# Incremental Recording
- Record in 5-minute chunks
- Allow playback of partial recordings
- Resume from last chunk if interrupted
```

## 10. Performance Considerations

### 10.1 Recording Performance
- Use hardware encoding if available (H.264 on device)
- Limit concurrent recordings per device (1 per device)
- Monitor device CPU/RAM during recording
- Implement graceful degradation if device overloaded

### 10.2 Encoding Performance
- Use FFmpeg with hardware acceleration (NVIDIA, Intel)
- Implement job queue for encoding tasks
- Prioritize encoding based on file size
- Cache encoding profiles

### 10.3 Storage Performance
- Use S3 multipart upload for large files
- Implement parallel chunk uploads
- Use CloudFront CDN for playback
- Cache frequently accessed videos

### 10.4 Playback Performance
- Implement adaptive bitrate streaming
- Use HTTP Range requests for seeking
- Cache video segments locally
- Implement progressive download

---

## 11. Security Considerations

### 11.1 Authentication & Authorization
- Validate JWT tokens on all endpoints
- Check device access permissions
- Implement role-based access control (RBAC)
- Log all access attempts

### 11.2 Data Protection
- Encrypt videos at rest (S3 encryption)
- Use HTTPS/TLS for all communications
- Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse
- Sanitize all user inputs

### 11.3 Sharing Security
- Generate cryptographically secure share tokens
- Implement optional password protection
- Set expiration on share links
- Log all share access

### 11.4 Audit Trail
- Log all recording operations
- Log all share creations/revocations
- Log all access to recordings
- Retain audit logs for 1 year

---

## 12. Monitoring & Observability

### 12.1 Prometheus Metrics
```
# Recording operations
recording_started_total{device_id}
recording_stopped_total{device_id}
recording_failed_total{device_id, error_type}
recording_duration_seconds{device_id}

# Video encoding
encoding_started_total
encoding_completed_total
encoding_failed_total
encoding_duration_seconds

# Storage operations
storage_upload_total{storage_type}
storage_upload_duration_seconds{storage_type}
storage_download_total
storage_usage_bytes{user_id}

# Playback
playback_started_total
playback_duration_seconds
playback_errors_total

# API
recording_api_requests_total{endpoint, method, status}
recording_api_request_duration_seconds{endpoint}
```

### 12.2 Logging
- Log all recording start/stop events
- Log all encoding operations
- Log all storage operations
- Log all API requests (with user ID)
- Log all access to recordings

### 12.3 Alerting
- Alert if recording fails for device
- Alert if encoding fails
- Alert if storage upload fails
- Alert if API response time exceeds threshold
- Alert if storage quota exceeded

---

## 13. Error Handling

### 13.1 Recording Errors
- Device disconnected: Pause recording, attempt resume
- ADB command failed: Retry up to 3 times
- Storage full: Stop recording, notify user
- Permission denied: Log error, notify user

### 13.2 Encoding Errors
- FFmpeg failed: Retry with different settings
- Corrupted video: Mark as failed, notify user
- Timeout: Kill process, mark as failed
- Out of disk space: Clean up temp files, retry

### 13.3 Storage Errors
- Upload failed: Retry with exponential backoff
- Quota exceeded: Reject new recordings
- Connection timeout: Retry with longer timeout
- Authentication failed: Log error, alert admin

### 13.4 Playback Errors
- Video not found: Return 404
- Permission denied: Return 403
- Streaming failed: Retry with different bitrate
- Seek failed: Return error, allow retry

---

## 14. Testing Strategy

### 14.1 Unit Tests
- Test RecordingManager with mocked ADB
- Test VideoEncoder with sample videos
- Test StorageManager with mocked S3
- Test PlaybackManager with sample metadata
- Target: 80%+ code coverage

### 14.2 Integration Tests
- Test end-to-end recording flow

- Test playback streaming
- Test sharing functionality

### 14.3 Performance Tests
- Load test with 50+ concurrent recordings
- Stress test encoding with large files
- Test storage with high throughput
- Test playback with many concurrent users
- Measure latency and throughput

### 14.4 Security Tests
- Test JWT validation
- Test permission checks
- Test rate limiting
- Test input validation
- Test share token security

## 15. Deployment Strategy

### 15.1 Environment Configuration
```
Development:
- Local filesystem storage
- Local PostgreSQL
- Mock device service
- FFmpeg installed locally

Staging:
- MinIO storage
- Staging PostgreSQL
- Real device service
- FFmpeg in container

Production:
- AWS S3 storage
- Production PostgreSQL (replicated)
- Production device service
- FFmpeg in container with GPU support
```

### 15.2 Deployment Process
1. Build Docker image with FFmpeg
2. Run tests in container
3. Push to container registry
4. Deploy to staging
5. Run smoke tests
6. Deploy to production (blue-green)
7. Monitor for errors

---

# Implementation Tasks: Screen Recording & Playback

**Feature ID:** SRP-001  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-16  
**Status:** Ready for Implementation

---

## Overview

This document breaks down the Screen Recording & Playback feature into actionable implementation tasks organized by phase and component.

**Total Estimated Effort:** 6-7 weeks  
**Team Size:** 3-4 developers  
**Priority:** High

---

## Phase 1: Core Recording Infrastructure (Week 1-2)

### Task SRP-1.1: Project Setup and Infrastructure
**Estimated:** 2 days  
**Owner:** DevOps/Backend Lead

**Description:**
Set up the recording service project structure, dependencies, and infrastructure.

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Create Go project structure following company standards
- [ ] Set up module dependencies (Gin, gRPC, PostgreSQL driver, S3 client, FFmpeg wrapper)
- [ ] Create Dockerfile with FFmpeg support
- [ ] Create docker-compose.yml with PostgreSQL and MinIO
- [ ] Set up PostgreSQL instance
- [ ] Set up S3/MinIO storage
- [ ] Configure environment variables and config management
- [ ] Set up logging infrastructure (zap/logrus)
- [ ] Set up Prometheus metrics exporter
- [ ] Create CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions)
- [ ] Set up code linting and formatting (golangci-lint)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Project builds successfully
- [ ] Docker containers start without errors
- [ ] All dependencies installed and working
- [ ] S3/MinIO accessible
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline runs on every commit
- [ ] Logging and metrics working

**Dependencies:** None

---

### Task SRP-1.2: Database Schema and Migrations
**Estimated:** 2 days  
**Owner:** Backend Developer

**Description:**
Create PostgreSQL schema for recording metadata storage.

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Create recordings table
- [ ] Create recording_metadata table
- [ ] Create recording_bookmarks table
- [ ] Create recording_shares table
- [ ] Create recording_access_log table with partitioning
- [ ] Create indexes for common queries
- [ ] Set up automatic partition creation for access_log
- [ ] Create migration scripts (golang-migrate)
- [ ] Add seed data for testing
- [ ] Document schema design
- [ ] Test migration rollback

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All tables created successfully
- [ ] Partitioning working correctly
- [ ] Indexes created and optimized
- [ ] Migrations can be applied and rolled back
- [ ] Schema documentation complete

**Dependencies:** Task SRP-1.1

---

### Task SRP-1.3: ADB Screen Recording Integration
**Estimated:** 3 days  
**Owner:** Backend Developer

**Description:**
Implement screen recording via ADB with device management.

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Create ADB client wrapper for screen recording
- [ ] Implement start recording command (adb shell screenrecord)
- [ ] Implement stop recording command
- [ ] Implement pause/resume functionality
- [ ] Implement file pull from device (adb pull)
- [ ] Add error handling and retry logic
- [ ] Add connection pooling for ADB
- [ ] Implement device disconnection handling
- [ ] Add unit tests for ADB operations
- [ ] Add integration tests with real device

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Recording starts and stops successfully
- [ ] Video files pulled from device correctly
- [ ] Error handling working (retries, timeouts)
- [ ] Device disconnection handled gracefully
- [ ] Unit tests pass (80%+ coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests pass with real device
- [ ] Recording impact < 5% CPU

**Dependencies:** Task SRP-1.1

---

**Estimated:** 2 days  
**Owner:** Backend Developer

**Description:**
Implement storage of recording metadata in PostgreSQL.

**Subtasks:**

- [ ] Implement create recording
- [ ] Implement update recording status

- [ ] Implement delete recording (soft delete)
- [ ] Implement storage quota checking
- [ ] Add connection pooling for database

- [ ] Add unit tests for storage operations
- [ ] Add integration tests with real database

**Acceptance Criteria:**

# AUDIRA GRID - Feature Implementation Roadmap

**Last Updated:** 2026-05-16  
**Status:** In Progress

---

## Overview

Roadmap implementasi 10 fitur baru untuk AUDIRA GRID Android Device Farm platform. Fitur-fitur ini diprioritaskan berdasarkan business value dan technical dependencies.

---

## Priority 1: Core Monitoring & Recording (Weeks 1-7)

### ✅ Feature 1: Device Performance Monitoring (DPM-001)
**Status:** Spec Complete - Ready for Implementation  
**Duration:** 6-7 weeks  
**Team Size:** 3-4 developers  
**Effort:** 28 tasks

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ requirements.md - Comprehensive functional and non-functional requirements
- ✅ design.md - Architecture, data flow, and technical design
- ✅ tasks.md - Detailed implementation tasks with dependencies

**Key Features:**
- Real-time CPU, RAM, battery, temperature monitoring
- Historical data tracking with 90-day retention
- Alert system with configurable thresholds
- Performance reports (PDF, CSV, JSON)
- Multi-device comparison view
- WebSocket streaming for real-time updates

**Success Metrics:**
- Performance data latency < 2 seconds
- Support 100+ concurrent devices
- Alert delivery within 5 seconds
- 99.9% uptime

**Start Date:** Week 1  
**End Date:** Week 7

---

### ✅ Feature 2: Screen Recording & Playback (SRP-001)
**Status:** Spec Complete - Ready for Implementation  
**Duration:** 6-7 weeks  
**Team Size:** 3-4 developers  
**Effort:** 31 tasks

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ requirements.md - Comprehensive functional and non-functional requirements
- ✅ design.md - Architecture, data flow, and technical design
- ✅ tasks.md - Detailed implementation tasks with dependencies

**Key Features:**
- Screen recording via ADB with configurable quality
- Video playback with full controls
- Recording management and filtering
- Sharing with secure tokens and expiration
- Bookmarks and annotations
- Storage management (local/S3)

**Success Metrics:**
- Recording reliability 99.5% (no dropped frames)
- Support 50+ concurrent recordings
- Playback startup < 2 seconds
- Storage efficiency < 50MB per minute

**Start Date:** Week 1  
**End Date:** Week 7

---

## Priority 2: Advanced Testing & Automation (Weeks 8-14)

**Status:** Pending Requirements  
**Duration:** 6-7 weeks  
**Team Size:** 3-4 developers

**Planned Features:**
- Record user interactions (taps, swipes, text input)

- Test result reporting

## Implementation Timeline

```
Week 1-7:   Feature 1 (DPM) + Feature 2 (SRP) - Parallel
Week 8-14:  Feature 3 (ATF) + Feature 4 (MDC) - Parallel

Week 22-28: Feature 8 (DGS) + Feature 9 (APP) + Feature 10 (STA) - Parallel

Total Duration: ~7 months (28 weeks)
Total Effort: ~250+ tasks
Team Size: 3-4 core developers + specialists as needed
```

## Resource Allocation

### Phase 1 (Weeks 1-7): Core Team
- **Backend Developers:** 2-3 (Go, PostgreSQL, Redis)
- **Frontend Developers:** 1-2 (React, Tauri)
- **DevOps Engineer:** 1 (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD)
- **QA Engineer:** 1 (Testing, automation)

### Phase 2 (Weeks 8-14): Core Team + Specialists
- **Backend Developers:** 2-3
- **Frontend Developers:** 1-2

### Phase 3 (Weeks 15-21): Core Team + ML Specialist

- **Frontend Developers:** 1
- **ML Engineer:** 1 (for Feature 5)

### Phase 4 (Weeks 22-28): Core Team
- **Backend Developers:** 2-3
- **Frontend Developers:** 1
- **DevOps Engineer:** 1

## Dependencies Between Features

```
Feature 1 (DPM)
    ├─► Provides device health data
    └─► Used by Feature 9 (APP)

    ├─► Provides session recordings
    └─► Used by Feature 3 (ATF)

Feature 8 (DGS)
    ├─► Independent

    ├─► Depends on Feature 1 (DPM)
    └─► Complements Feature 1

Feature 10 (STA)
    ├─► Depends on Feature 3 (ATF)
    └─► Orchestrates other features
```

---

## Risk Assessment

### High Risk
- **Feature 5 (AOD):** Requires ML expertise, complex integration
- **Feature 7 (NTM):** Network traffic capture may have platform limitations
- **Feature 9 (APP):** Requires deep Android profiling knowledge

### Medium Risk
- **Feature 3 (ATF):** Complex state management for test recording/replay
- **Feature 4 (MDC):** Synchronization challenges across multiple devices
- **Feature 8 (DGS):** GPS spoofing may have Android version limitations

### Low Risk
- **Feature 1 (DPM):** Well-defined requirements, standard monitoring patterns
- **Feature 2 (SRP):** Standard video recording/playback, proven technologies
- **Feature 6 (RFM):** Standard file operations via ADB
- **Feature 10 (STA):** Standard task scheduling patterns

input.text:
# Feature Requirements: Automated Testing Framework

**Feature ID:** ATF-001  
**Priority:** High  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-16  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
Automated testing framework that enables users to record user interactions on Android devices, replay recorded sessions, create parameterized test scripts, and integrate with CI/CD pipelines for continuous testing.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Enable non-technical users to create automated tests through recording
- Reduce manual testing effort and time-to-market
- Improve test coverage and consistency
- Enable continuous testing in CI/CD pipelines
- Provide test result reporting and analytics
- Support regression testing across device models

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- Record/replay accuracy > 99%
- Test execution time < 2x manual execution
- Support 100+ concurrent test executions
- Test result reporting within 5 minutes
- 99.5% test reliability (no flaky tests)

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 Test Recording

#### 2.1.1 Recording Initiation
**REQ-ATF-001:** Users SHALL be able to start test recording from:
- Device detail view (one-click start button)
- Test management interface
- Programmatic API

**REQ-ATF-002:** Recording start SHALL capture:
- Initial device state (app, screen orientation)
- Timestamp of recording start
- Device metadata (model, OS version)
- User who initiated recording

**REQ-ATF-003:** The system SHALL support recording with configurable options:
- Target app (optional, for app-specific testing)
- Recording timeout (default 30 minutes)
- Screenshot capture interval (default 1 second)
- Gesture capture (taps, swipes, long-press, pinch)
- Text input capture
- System event capture (back button, home button)

#### 2.1.2 Interaction Capture
**REQ-ATF-004:** The system SHALL capture the following user interactions:
- Tap (single, double, long-press)
- Swipe (direction, distance, duration)
- Pinch (zoom in/out)
- Text input (keyboard input, paste)
- System buttons (back, home, recent apps)
- App transitions
- Screen orientation changes
- Dialog/popup interactions

**REQ-ATF-005:** For each interaction, the system SHALL record:
- Interaction type
- Coordinates (x, y)
- Timestamp (relative to recording start)
- Duration (for long-press, swipe)
- Associated text (for text input)
- Screen state before and after

**REQ-ATF-006:** The system SHALL capture screenshots at configurable intervals:
- Default: 1 screenshot per second
- Configurable: 0.5 to 5 seconds
- On-demand: Capture on significant events
- Compression: Store as JPEG with configurable quality

**REQ-ATF-007:** The system SHALL detect and record:
- App crashes and ANRs
- Network errors
- Permission dialogs
- System notifications
- Toast messages
- Logcat errors

#### 2.1.3 Recording Control
**REQ-ATF-008:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Pause recording (interactions not captured during pause)
- Resume recording (continues from pause point)
- Stop recording (finalizes test script)
- Cancel recording (discards unsaved script)

**REQ-ATF-009:** Recording duration limit SHALL be configurable:
- Default: 30 minutes
- Maximum: 120 minutes
- Automatic stop when limit reached

**REQ-ATF-010:** If device disconnects during recording:
- Recording SHALL pause automatically
- System SHALL attempt to resume when device reconnects
- If reconnection fails within 5 minutes, recording SHALL be finalized

### 2.2 Test Script Generation

#### 2.2.1 Script Creation
**REQ-ATF-011:** After recording stops, the system SHALL automatically generate a test script containing:
- Sequence of recorded interactions
- Screenshots and expected results
- Assertions (optional, user-defined)
- Wait conditions (implicit waits between interactions)
- Error handling (on crash, ANR, etc.)

**REQ-ATF-012:** The system SHALL support multiple script formats:
- YAML (human-readable, editable)
- JSON (structured, programmatic)
- Python (executable, with Appium integration)
- Java (executable, with Appium integration)

**REQ-ATF-013:** Generated scripts SHALL include:
- Metadata (device, app, date, duration)
- Setup steps (app launch, initial state)
- Test steps (recorded interactions)
- Teardown steps (cleanup)
- Expected results and assertions

**REQ-ATF-014:** The system SHALL support script editing:
- Add/remove/modify interactions
- Add assertions and validations
- Add wait conditions
- Add conditional logic (if/else)
- Add loops (for, while)
- Add variables and parameterization

#### 2.2.2 Script Validation
**REQ-ATF-015:** The system SHALL validate scripts before execution:
- Check syntax correctness
- Verify all referenced elements exist
- Check for circular dependencies
- Validate assertions
- Warn about potential issues (long waits, flaky interactions)

**REQ-ATF-016:** The system SHALL provide script validation feedback:
- List of errors and warnings
- Suggestions for fixes
- Severity levels (error, warning, info)

### 2.3 Test Execution

#### 2.3.1 Execution Modes
**REQ-ATF-017:** The system SHALL support multiple execution modes:
- **Single execution:** Run test once on selected device
- **Multi-device execution:** Run test on multiple devices in parallel
- **Batch execution:** Run multiple tests sequentially or in parallel
- **Scheduled execution:** Run tests at specific times (daily, weekly, etc.)
- **CI/CD integration:** Trigger from CI/CD pipeline

**REQ-ATF-018:** Users SHALL be able to configure execution parameters:
- Target device(s)
- Test data/parameters
- Timeout values
- Retry policy (number of retries on failure)
- Screenshot capture (on/off)
- Video recording (on/off)

#### 2.3.2 Test Execution
**REQ-ATF-019:** During test execution, the system SHALL:
- Execute interactions in recorded sequence
- Wait for expected results (implicit waits)
- Capture screenshots at configured intervals
- Record video of execution (optional)
- Monitor for crashes, ANRs, errors
- Collect performance metrics (optional)

**REQ-ATF-020:** The system SHALL implement intelligent waits:
- Implicit wait: Default wait between interactions (configurable, default 5 seconds)
- Explicit wait: Wait for specific element/condition
- Adaptive wait: Adjust wait time based on device performance

**REQ-ATF-021:** The system SHALL handle test failures gracefully:
- Capture screenshot on failure
- Record video segment around failure
- Collect device logs (logcat)
- Attempt recovery (retry interaction, restart app)
- Mark test as failed with detailed error message

**REQ-ATF-022:** The system SHALL support test parameterization:
- Data-driven testing (CSV, JSON data sources)
- Multiple test runs with different parameters
- Parameter substitution in interactions (e.g., text input)
- Result aggregation across parameterized runs

#### 2.3.3 Execution Monitoring
**REQ-ATF-023:** During execution, users SHALL be able to:
- View real-time execution progress
- See current step being executed
- View live device screen
- Pause/resume execution
- Stop execution
- View execution logs

**REQ-ATF-024:** The system SHALL provide execution metrics:
- Total execution time
- Time per step
- Success/failure rate
- Device performance during execution
- Network activity (if monitored)

### 2.4 Test Results and Reporting

#### 2.4.1 Result Collection
**REQ-ATF-025:** After test execution, the system SHALL collect:
- Test status (passed, failed, skipped)
- Execution duration
- Screenshots (before/after each step)
- Video recording (if enabled)
- Device logs (logcat)
- Performance metrics (CPU, RAM, battery)
- Errors and exceptions

**REQ-ATF-026:** The system SHALL store test results with:
- Test ID and name
- Device information
- Execution timestamp
- Test parameters (if parameterized)
- Pass/fail status
- Failure reason (if failed)
- Execution duration
- Screenshots and video

#### 2.4.2 Result Analysis
**REQ-ATF-027:** The system SHALL provide result analysis:
- Pass/fail rate across multiple runs
- Failure trend analysis
- Device-specific failure patterns
- Performance trend analysis
- Flaky test detection

**REQ-ATF-028:** The system SHALL detect flaky tests:
- Tests that pass/fail inconsistently
- Identify root causes (timing, device state, etc.)
- Suggest fixes (increase waits, add assertions)

#### 2.4.3 Result Reporting
**REQ-ATF-029:** The system SHALL generate test reports in multiple formats:
- HTML (interactive, with screenshots/videos)
- PDF (printable, summary view)
- JSON (structured, for programmatic access)
- JUnit XML (for CI/CD integration)

**REQ-ATF-030:** Reports SHALL include:
- Test summary (total, passed, failed, skipped)
- Detailed results per test
- Screenshots and videos
- Device information
- Execution timeline
- Performance metrics
- Failure analysis

**REQ-ATF-031:** The system SHALL support report customization:
- Select which tests to include
- Choose report format
- Configure detail level (summary, detailed, full)
- Add custom sections/notes

### 2.5 CI/CD Integration

#### 2.5.1 Pipeline Integration
**REQ-ATF-032:** The system SHALL integrate with CI/CD pipelines:
- Trigger tests from CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, etc.)
- Pass test parameters via environment variables
- Report results back to CI/CD
- Fail build on test failure (configurable)

**REQ-ATF-033:** The system SHALL provide CI/CD APIs:
- REST API for triggering test execution
- Webhook support for CI/CD events
- Result polling API
- Report download API

**REQ-ATF-034:** The system SHALL support CI/CD best practices:
- Parallel test execution
- Test result aggregation
- Artifact storage (screenshots, videos, logs)
- Notification on test failure
- Test history and trends

#### 2.5.2 Test Management
**REQ-ATF-035:** The system SHALL support test management:
- Organize tests into suites
- Tag tests for filtering
- Version control for test scripts
- Test dependencies (test A must run before test B)
- Test scheduling

**REQ-ATF-036:** The system SHALL provide test discovery:
- Automatically discover tests in repository
- Support multiple test formats
- Validate discovered tests

### 2.6 Test Maintenance

#### 2.6.1 Script Maintenance
**REQ-ATF-037:** The system SHALL support script maintenance:
- Update recorded interactions
- Re-record specific steps
- Merge multiple recordings
- Clone and modify existing tests
- Archive old tests

**REQ-ATF-038:** The system SHALL detect and handle UI changes:
- Detect when recorded elements no longer exist
- Suggest alternative locators
- Provide visual diff of UI changes
- Allow manual element re-identification

#### 2.6.2 Test Debugging
**REQ-ATF-039:** The system SHALL provide debugging capabilities:
- Step-by-step execution with breakpoints
- Variable inspection
- Interaction replay (slow-motion)
- Screenshot comparison (expected vs. actual)
- Logcat filtering and search

**REQ-ATF-040:** The system SHALL support test troubleshooting:
- Identify flaky interactions
- Suggest wait time adjustments
- Detect timing issues
- Recommend best practices

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-ATF-NFR-001:** Test execution SHALL NOT impact device performance by more than 10% CPU usage.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-002:** The system SHALL support 100+ concurrent test executions.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-003:** Test result reporting SHALL complete within 5 minutes for 100+ tests.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-004:** Screenshot capture SHALL not exceed 100ms per screenshot.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-005:** Test script generation SHALL complete within 30 seconds.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-ATF-NFR-006:** The system SHALL scale to support 1000+ test scripts.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-007:** Database queries for test results SHALL return within 3 seconds for 100,000+ results.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-008:** Storage for test artifacts (screenshots, videos) SHALL scale to petabyte-scale.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-ATF-NFR-009:** Test execution reliability SHALL be 99.5% (no unexpected failures).

**REQ-ATF-NFR-010:** Test scripts SHALL be version-controlled and recoverable.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-011:** Failed test executions SHALL be automatically retried up to 3 times.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-012:** Test results SHALL be persisted immediately after execution.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-ATF-NFR-013:** All test APIs SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-014:** Test scripts SHALL NOT contain sensitive data (passwords, tokens).

**REQ-ATF-NFR-015:** Test results SHALL be encrypted at rest.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-016:** Access to test scripts and results SHALL be logged for audit purposes.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-ATF-NFR-017:** Test framework SHALL be implemented as a separate microservice in Go.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-018:** Codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-019:** System SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-ATF-NFR-020:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Device Service Integration
**REQ-ATF-INT-001:** Test framework SHALL integrate with device-service for device management.

**REQ-ATF-INT-002:** Device-service SHALL provide device connection status and metadata.

### 4.2 Recording Service Integration
**REQ-ATF-INT-003:** Test framework SHALL integrate with recording-service for video capture.

**REQ-ATF-INT-004:** Recording-service SHALL provide video files and metadata.

### 4.3 Stream Service Integration
**REQ-ATF-INT-005:** Test framework MAY integrate with stream-service for live monitoring.

### 4.4 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-ATF-INT-006:** All test APIs SHALL be exposed through api-gateway.

**REQ-ATF-INT-007:** API-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting for test endpoints.

### 4.5 Auth Service Integration
**REQ-ATF-INT-008:** Test framework SHALL validate JWT tokens via auth-service.

---

## 5. User Interface Requirements

### 5.1 Test Recording UI
**REQ-ATF-UI-001:** Recording interface SHALL display:
- Recording status and duration
- Interaction count
- Device screen preview
- Recording controls (pause, resume, stop, cancel)
- Settings button

### 5.2 Test Script Management UI
**REQ-ATF-UI-002:** Script management interface SHALL display:
- List of test scripts with metadata
- Search and filtering
- Script editor
- Script validation results
- Version history

### 5.3 Test Execution UI
**REQ-ATF-UI-003:** Execution interface SHALL display:
- Test selection and configuration
- Execution progress
- Live device screen
- Execution logs
- Execution controls (pause, stop)

### 5.4 Test Results UI
**REQ-ATF-UI-004:** Results interface SHALL display:
- Test results summary
- Detailed results per test
- Screenshots and videos
- Failure analysis
- Report generation options

---

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 test_scripts Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE test_scripts (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    device_id UUID REFERENCES devices(id),
    app_package VARCHAR(255),
    format VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'yaml', 'json', 'python', 'java'
    script_content TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    deleted_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

#### 6.1.2 test_executions Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE test_executions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    test_script_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES test_scripts(id),
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'running', 'passed', 'failed', 'skipped'
    duration_seconds INTEGER,
    started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    completed_at TIMESTAMP,
    error_message TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.3 test_results Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE test_results (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    execution_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES test_executions(id),
    step_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    step_name VARCHAR(255),
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'passed', 'failed', 'skipped'
    duration_ms INTEGER,
    screenshot_path TEXT,
    error_message TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

---

## 7. API Requirements

### 7.1 REST API Endpoints

```
POST /api/v1/tests/record/start
POST /api/v1/tests/record/{recording_id}/stop
POST /api/v1/tests/record/{recording_id}/pause
POST /api/v1/tests/record/{recording_id}/resume

GET /api/v1/tests/scripts
POST /api/v1/tests/scripts
GET /api/v1/tests/scripts/{script_id}
PUT /api/v1/tests/scripts/{script_id}
DELETE /api/v1/tests/scripts/{script_id}

POST /api/v1/tests/execute
GET /api/v1/tests/executions/{execution_id}
GET /api/v1/tests/results/{execution_id}

GET /api/v1/tests/reports/{execution_id}
```

---

## 8. Acceptance Criteria

### 8.1 Feature Complete Criteria
- [ ] All functional requirements implemented
- [ ] All non-functional requirements met
- [ ] UI components implemented and responsive
- [ ] Database schema deployed
- [ ] API documentation completed
- [ ] Unit tests achieve 80%+ coverage
- [ ] Integration tests cover critical flows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met

### 8.2 User Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can record test interactions
- [ ] Generated test scripts are executable
- [ ] Tests can be executed on multiple devices
- [ ] Test results are accurate and reliable
- [ ] Reports are generated correctly
- [ ] CI/CD integration working

---

## 9. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|

input.text:
# Feature Requirements: Multi-device Sync Control

**Feature ID:** MDC-001  
**Priority:** High  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-16  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
Multi-device synchronization and control feature that enables users to control multiple Android devices simultaneously with synchronized input, group management, and broadcast commands for coordinated testing and demonstrations.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Enable synchronized testing across multiple device models
- Support coordinated demonstrations and presentations
- Reduce testing time for multi-device scenarios
- Enable group-based device management
- Support broadcast commands to device groups
- Improve testing efficiency for cross-device compatibility

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- Input synchronization latency < 500ms across devices
- Support 50+ concurrent synchronized devices
- Command broadcast to 50 devices < 2 seconds
- 99.5% command delivery reliability
- Sync accuracy > 99%

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 Device Grouping

#### 2.1.1 Group Management
**REQ-MDC-001:** Users SHALL be able to create device groups:
- Group name and description
- Add/remove devices from group
- Set group properties (sync mode, broadcast settings)
- Save group configurations as presets

**REQ-MDC-002:** The system SHALL support group types:
- **Manual groups:** User-selected devices
- **Dynamic groups:** Based on device properties (model, OS version, etc.)
- **Preset groups:** Pre-configured for common scenarios

**REQ-MDC-003:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Create new groups
- Edit existing groups
- Delete groups
- Clone groups
- Share groups with other users (future)

**REQ-MDC-004:** The system SHALL support group hierarchy:
- Parent groups containing sub-groups
- Nested group operations
- Group inheritance of settings

#### 2.1.2 Device Selection
**REQ-MDC-005:** Users SHALL be able to select devices for synchronization:
- Individual device selection
- Group selection
- Filter by device properties (model, OS, status)
- Quick select (all online, all offline, etc.)

**REQ-MDC-006:** The system SHALL display device status in groups:
- Online/offline status
- Battery level
- Current app
- Performance metrics

### 2.2 Synchronized Input

#### 2.2.1 Input Synchronization
**REQ-MDC-007:** The system SHALL support synchronized input across devices:
- Tap (single, double, long-press)
- Swipe (direction, distance, duration)
- Pinch (zoom in/out)
- Text input (keyboard input, paste)
- System buttons (back, home, recent apps)

**REQ-MDC-008:** Input synchronization SHALL work in two modes:
- **Mirror mode:** All devices receive identical input at same coordinates
- **Relative mode:** Input adjusted based on device screen size/resolution

**REQ-MDC-009:** The system SHALL handle input synchronization:
- Capture input from primary device (master)
- Broadcast to all devices in group (slaves)
- Synchronize timing across devices (< 500ms latency)
- Handle device disconnection during sync

**REQ-MDC-010:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Select primary device (master)
- Switch primary device during session
- Pause synchronization temporarily
- Resume synchronization
- Stop synchronization

#### 2.2.2 Coordinate Mapping
**REQ-MDC-011:** The system SHALL support coordinate mapping:
- Map coordinates from master device to slave devices
- Handle different screen resolutions
- Handle different screen orientations
- Handle notches and safe areas

**REQ-MDC-012:** Coordinate mapping modes:
- **Absolute:** Use exact coordinates (for same-resolution devices)
- **Proportional:** Scale coordinates based on screen size
- **Element-based:** Map to UI elements (requires element detection)

**REQ-MDC-013:** The system SHALL provide coordinate mapping configuration:
- Select mapping mode per group
- Manual coordinate adjustment
- Calibration tool for accurate mapping

#### 2.2.3 Synchronization Control
**REQ-MDC-014:** Users SHALL be able to configure synchronization:
- Sync delay (default 0ms, configurable 0-5000ms)
- Retry policy on sync failure
- Timeout for sync operations
- Partial sync (skip failed devices)

**REQ-MDC-015:** The system SHALL handle synchronization failures:
- Retry failed devices up to 3 times
- Log failed synchronizations
- Notify user of sync failures
- Option to continue or abort

### 2.3 Broadcast Commands

#### 2.3.1 Command Broadcasting
**REQ-MDC-016:** The system SHALL support broadcast commands:
- App launch (same app on all devices)
- App close
- Device restart
- Screen on/off
- Volume control
- Screenshot capture
- Custom shell commands (with restrictions)

**REQ-MDC-017:** Broadcast commands SHALL:
- Execute on all devices in group simultaneously
- Report execution status per device
- Handle partial failures (some devices succeed, some fail)
- Support command queuing

**REQ-MDC-018:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Create custom broadcast commands
- Save command templates
- Schedule broadcast commands
- Execute broadcast commands with confirmation

#### 2.3.2 Command Execution
**REQ-MDC-019:** The system SHALL execute broadcast commands:
- In parallel across devices (for speed)
- Sequentially (for resource management)
- With configurable concurrency

**REQ-MDC-020:** Command execution SHALL include:
- Execution status per device (success, failure, timeout)
- Execution duration
- Error messages
- Command output (if applicable)

**REQ-MDC-021:** The system SHALL support command chaining:
- Execute multiple commands in sequence
- Conditional execution (if previous succeeded)
- Wait for completion before next command

### 2.4 Synchronized Monitoring

#### 2.4.1 Real-time Monitoring
**REQ-MDC-022:** The system SHALL provide synchronized monitoring:
- Display all devices in group simultaneously
- Show device screens side-by-side
- Synchronized scrolling across devices
- Synchronized video playback

**REQ-MDC-023:** Users SHALL be able to:
- View all device screens in grid layout
- Adjust grid layout (2x2, 3x3, etc.)
- Focus on individual device
- Compare device states

**REQ-MDC-024:** The system SHALL display synchronized metrics:
- Performance metrics (CPU, RAM, battery)
- Network activity
- App state
- User interactions

#### 2.4.2 Comparison View
**REQ-MDC-025:** The system SHALL provide comparison view:
- Side-by-side device comparison
- Highlight differences between devices
- Screenshot comparison (expected vs. actual)
- Performance comparison

**REQ-MDC-026:** Comparison features:
- Visual diff of UI elements
- Performance metrics comparison
- Behavior comparison (same input, different results)
- Regression detection

### 2.5 Session Management

#### 2.5.1 Session Control
**REQ-MDC-027:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Start synchronization session
- Pause session (pause input sync, keep monitoring)
- Resume session
- End session
- Save session for later replay

**REQ-MDC-028:** Session metadata SHALL include:
- Session name and description
- Devices in session
- Start/end time
- Duration
- Commands executed
- Issues encountered

**REQ-MDC-029:** The system SHALL support session recording:
- Record all interactions and commands
- Record device screens (video)
- Record performance metrics
- Record errors and issues

#### 2.5.2 Session Replay
**REQ-MDC-030:** Users SHALL be able to replay sessions:
- Replay recorded interactions
- Replay at different speeds
- Pause and resume replay
- Step through interactions
- Compare replay with live execution

**REQ-MDC-031:** Session replay SHALL include:
- Synchronized playback across all devices
- Performance metrics overlay
- Interaction timeline
- Error highlighting

### 2.6 Conflict Resolution

#### 2.6.1 Conflict Detection
**REQ-MDC-032:** The system SHALL detect synchronization conflicts:
- Device becomes unavailable during sync
- Input fails on some devices
- Devices in different states
- Network latency issues

**REQ-MDC-033:** Conflict detection SHALL:
- Identify affected devices
- Log conflict details
- Notify user
- Suggest resolution

#### 2.6.2 Conflict Resolution
**REQ-MDC-034:** The system SHALL support conflict resolution strategies:
- **Abort:** Stop synchronization, notify user
- **Retry:** Retry failed operations
- **Skip:** Skip failed devices, continue with others
- **Manual:** Let user decide action

**REQ-MDC-035:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Configure default resolution strategy
- Override strategy per operation
- Manually resolve conflicts

### 2.7 Performance Optimization

#### 2.7.1 Latency Optimization
**REQ-MDC-036:** The system SHALL optimize synchronization latency:
- Batch commands for efficiency
- Parallel execution where possible
- Network optimization (compression, caching)
- Device-side optimization

**REQ-MDC-037:** The system SHALL provide latency monitoring:
- Measure sync latency per device
- Identify bottlenecks
- Suggest optimizations
- Alert on high latency

#### 2.7.2 Bandwidth Optimization
**REQ-MDC-038:** The system SHALL optimize bandwidth usage:
- Compress data transfers
- Cache device state
- Minimize screen updates
- Throttle updates based on network conditions

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-MDC-NFR-001:** Input synchronization latency SHALL be < 500ms across devices.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-002:** The system SHALL support 50+ concurrent synchronized devices.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-003:** Broadcast commands SHALL execute on 50 devices within 2 seconds.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-004:** Screen updates SHALL be synchronized within 100ms.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-005:** Command execution SHALL not impact device performance by more than 5% CPU.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-MDC-NFR-006:** The system SHALL scale to support 1000+ device groups.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-007:** Database queries for group management SHALL return within 2 seconds.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-008:** Session storage SHALL scale to support 10,000+ recorded sessions.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-MDC-NFR-009:** Command delivery reliability SHALL be 99.5%.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-010:** Synchronization accuracy SHALL be > 99%.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-011:** Failed commands SHALL be automatically retried up to 3 times.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-012:** Session data SHALL be persisted immediately after session end.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-MDC-NFR-013:** All sync APIs SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-014:** Broadcast commands SHALL be restricted to safe operations.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-015:** Session recordings SHALL be encrypted at rest.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-016:** Access to sessions and groups SHALL be logged for audit.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-MDC-NFR-017:** Sync service SHALL be implemented as separate microservice in Go.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-018:** Codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-019:** System SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-MDC-NFR-020:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Device Service Integration
**REQ-MDC-INT-001:** Sync service SHALL integrate with device-service for device management.

**REQ-MDC-INT-002:** Device-service SHALL provide device status and metadata.

### 4.2 Stream Service Integration
**REQ-MDC-INT-003:** Sync service SHALL integrate with stream-service for screen streaming.

**REQ-MDC-INT-004:** Stream-service SHALL provide synchronized screen updates.

### 4.3 Recording Service Integration
**REQ-MDC-INT-005:** Sync service MAY integrate with recording-service for session recording.

### 4.4 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-MDC-INT-006:** All sync APIs SHALL be exposed through api-gateway.

**REQ-MDC-INT-007:** API-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting for sync endpoints.

### 4.5 Auth Service Integration
**REQ-MDC-INT-008:** Sync service SHALL validate JWT tokens via auth-service.

---

## 5. User Interface Requirements

### 5.1 Group Management UI
**REQ-MDC-UI-001:** Group management interface SHALL display:
- List of groups with metadata
- Device list per group
- Group creation/edit dialog
- Group deletion confirmation

### 5.2 Synchronization UI
**REQ-MDC-UI-002:** Synchronization interface SHALL display:
- Primary device (master) selection
- Slave devices list
- Sync status and latency
- Sync controls (start, pause, stop)

### 5.3 Multi-device View
**REQ-MDC-UI-003:** Multi-device view SHALL display:
- Grid of device screens
- Configurable grid layout
- Device status indicators
- Synchronized controls

### 5.4 Session Management UI
**REQ-MDC-UI-004:** Session management interface SHALL display:
- List of recorded sessions
- Session metadata
- Session replay controls
- Session export options

---

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 device_groups Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE device_groups (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    group_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'manual', 'dynamic', 'preset'
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    deleted_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

#### 6.1.2 group_devices Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE group_devices (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    group_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES device_groups(id),
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'master', 'slave'
    added_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.3 sync_sessions Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE sync_sessions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    group_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES device_groups(id),
    name VARCHAR(255),
    started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    ended_at TIMESTAMP,
    duration_seconds INTEGER,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'active', 'paused', 'completed'
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id)
);
```

#### 6.1.4 sync_commands Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE sync_commands (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    session_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sync_sessions(id),
    command_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    command_data JSONB NOT NULL,
    executed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL -- 'success', 'partial', 'failed'
);
```

# Design Document: Automated Testing Framework

**Feature ID:** ATF-001  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-16  
**Status:** Draft

---

## 1. Architecture Overview

### 1.1 System Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Desktop App (Tauri)                       │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  React UI Components                                     │   │
│  │  - Test Recording Interface                              │   │
│  │  - Script Editor                                         │   │
│  │  - Test Execution UI                                     │   │
│  │  - Results & Reporting                                   │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ▲
                              │ HTTPS/WebSocket
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      API Gateway                                 │
│  - Authentication (JWT validation)                              │
│  - Rate limiting                                                │
│  - Request routing                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ▲
        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
        ▼                     ▼                     ▼
┌────────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐
│ Device         │    │ Recording      │    │ Test           │
│ Service        │    │ Service        │    │ Service        │
│ (gRPC)         │    │ (gRPC)         │    │ (gRPC)         │
└────────────────┘    └────────────────┘    └────────────────┘
        ▲                     ▲                     ▲
        └─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘
                              ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐

```

### 1.2 Component Responsibilities

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|-----------------|
| **Desktop App** | Display test recording, execution, and results UI |
| **API Gateway** | Route requests, validate auth, rate limiting |
| **Test Service** | Manage test scripts, execution, results |
| **Device Service** | Provide device connection and control |
| **Recording Service** | Capture video and screenshots |
| **PostgreSQL** | Store test scripts, results, metadata |
| **Redis** | Cache test data, execution queue |
| **S3/MinIO** | Store screenshots, videos, artifacts |

## 2. Test Service Architecture

### 2.1 Service Components

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Test Service (Go)                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ API Layer (gRPC + REST)                              │   │
│  │ - Recording endpoints                                │   │
│  │ - Script management endpoints                        │   │
│  │ - Execution endpoints                                │   │
│  │ - Results endpoints                                  │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Business Logic Layer                                 │   │
│  │ - RecordingManager                                   │   │
│  │ - ScriptGenerator                                    │   │
│  │ - ScriptValidator                                    │   │
│  │ - ExecutionEngine                                    │   │
│  │ - ResultAnalyzer                                     │   │
│  │ - ReportGenerator                                    │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Data Access Layer                                    │   │
│  │ - ScriptRepository                                   │   │
│  │ - ExecutionRepository                                │   │
│  │ - ResultRepository                                   │   │
│  │ - SessionRepository                                  │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Infrastructure Layer                                 │   │
│  │ - PostgreSQL driver                                  │   │
│  │ - Redis client                                       │   │

│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Key Classes/Interfaces

```go
// Test Recording
type RecordingManager interface {
    StartRecording(ctx context.Context, req *RecordingRequest) (*Recording, error)
    StopRecording(ctx context.Context, recordingID string) (*Recording, error)
    PauseRecording(ctx context.Context, recordingID string) error
    ResumeRecording(ctx context.Context, recordingID string) error
    CancelRecording(ctx context.Context, recordingID string) error
    GetRecording(ctx context.Context, recordingID string) (*Recording, error)
}

type Recording struct {
    ID              string
    DeviceID        string
    UserID          string
    Status          string // "recording", "processing", "ready"
    Interactions    []Interaction
    Screenshots     []Screenshot
    StartTime       time.Time
    EndTime         *time.Time
    DurationSeconds int32
}

type Interaction struct {
    Type        string // "tap", "swipe", "text_input", etc.
    Coordinates [2]int32
    Timestamp   int32 // relative to recording start
    Duration    int32
    Data        map[string]interface{}
}

// Script Generation
type ScriptGenerator interface {
    GenerateScript(ctx context.Context, recording *Recording, format string) (*TestScript, error)
    ValidateScript(ctx context.Context, script *TestScript) (*ValidationResult, error)
}

type TestScript struct {
    ID          string
    Name        string
    Description string
    Format      string // "yaml", "json", "python", "java"
    Content     string
    Metadata    ScriptMetadata
    CreatedAt   time.Time
    UpdatedAt   time.Time
}

type ScriptMetadata struct {
    DeviceModel    string
    AndroidVersion string
    AppPackage     string
    Duration       int32
    InteractionCount int32
}

// Test Execution
type ExecutionEngine interface {
    ExecuteScript(ctx context.Context, req *ExecutionRequest) (*Execution, error)
    GetExecution(ctx context.Context, executionID string) (*Execution, error)
    CancelExecution(ctx context.Context, executionID string) error
}

type ExecutionRequest struct {
    ScriptID      string
    DeviceIDs     []string
    Parameters    map[string]interface{}
    Timeout       int32
    RetryPolicy   RetryPolicy
    CaptureVideo  bool
}

type Execution struct {
    ID              string
    ScriptID        string
    DeviceID        string
    Status          string // "running", "passed", "failed"
    Results         []StepResult
    StartTime       time.Time
    EndTime         *time.Time
    DurationSeconds int32
    ErrorMessage    string
}

type StepResult struct {
    StepNumber    int32
    StepName      string
    Status        string // "passed", "failed"
    DurationMs    int32
    ScreenshotURL string
    ErrorMessage  string
}

// Result Analysis
type ResultAnalyzer interface {
    AnalyzeResults(ctx context.Context, executionID string) (*Analysis, error)
    DetectFlakyTests(ctx context.Context, scriptID string) (*FlakyTestReport, error)
}

type Analysis struct {
    PassRate       float64
    FailureReasons map[string]int
    PerformanceMetrics PerformanceMetrics
    Issues         []Issue
}

// Report Generation
type ReportGenerator interface {
    GenerateReport(ctx context.Context, req *ReportRequest) (*Report, error)
    ExportReport(ctx context.Context, reportID string, format string) ([]byte, error)
}

type ReportRequest struct {
    ExecutionIDs []string
    Format       string // "html", "pdf", "json", "junit"
    IncludeVideo bool
}
```

## 3. Data Flow

### 3.1 Test Recording Flow

```
User clicks "Start Recording"
    │
    ▼
RecordingManager.StartRecording()
    │
    ├─► Create Recording record in DB
    │
    ├─► Get device via device-service
    │
    ├─► Start interaction capture (ADB event monitoring)
    │
    ├─► Start screenshot capture (1 per second)
    │
    ├─► Broadcast recording started event
    │
    ▼
Recording in progress (user performs interactions)
    │
    ├─► Capture taps, swipes, text input
    │
    ├─► Capture screenshots
    │
    ├─► Monitor for crashes, ANRs
    │
    ▼
User clicks "Stop Recording"
    │
    ├─► Stop interaction capture
    │
    ├─► Stop screenshot capture
    │
    ├─► Update Recording status to "processing"
    │
    ├─► Queue script generation job
    │
    ▼
ScriptGenerator processes recording
    │
    ├─► Analyze interactions
    │
    ├─► Generate test script (YAML/JSON)
    │
    ├─► Add assertions and waits
    │
    ├─► Validate script
    │
    ├─► Store script in DB
    │
    ├─► Update Recording status to "ready"
    │
    ▼
Test script ready for execution
```

### 3.2 Test Execution Flow

```
User selects test script and devices
    │
    ▼
ExecutionEngine.ExecuteScript()
    │
    ├─► Create Execution record
    │
    ├─► Queue execution job per device
    │
    ▼
For each device:
    │
    ├─► Launch app (if specified)
    │
    ├─► Execute script steps sequentially
    │
    ├─► For each step:
    │   ├─► Capture screenshot (before)
    │   ├─► Execute interaction
    │   ├─► Wait for expected result
    │   ├─► Capture screenshot (after)
    │   ├─► Verify assertion (if exists)
    │   ├─► Record step result
    │
    ├─► Handle failures (retry, capture logs)
    │
    ├─► Collect performance metrics
    │
    ▼
ResultAnalyzer processes results
    │
    ├─► Analyze pass/fail rate
    │
    ├─► Detect flaky tests
    │
    ├─► Generate report
    │
    ▼
Test execution complete
```

### 3.3 Script Generation Flow

```
Recording completed
    │
    ▼
ScriptGenerator analyzes interactions
    │
    ├─► Group related interactions
    │
    ├─► Detect wait conditions
    │
    ├─► Generate assertions
    │
    ├─► Add error handling
    │
    ▼
Generate script in target format
    │
    ├─► YAML: Human-readable, editable
    │
    ├─► JSON: Structured, programmatic
    │
    ├─► Python: Executable with Appium
    │
    ├─► Java: Executable with Appium
    │
    ▼
Validate generated script
    │
    ├─► Check syntax
    │
    ├─► Verify interactions
    │
    ├─► Check assertions
    │
    ▼
Store script in database
```

---

## 4. Database Design

### 4.1 Schema Overview

```sql
-- Test scripts
test_scripts (
    id, name, description, device_id, app_package,
    format, script_content, created_by, created_at, updated_at
)

-- Test executions
test_executions (
    id, test_script_id, device_id, status,
    duration_seconds, started_at, completed_at, error_message
)

-- Test results (per step)
test_results (
    id, execution_id, step_number, step_name, status,
    duration_ms, screenshot_path, error_message
)

-- Test recordings
test_recordings (
    id, device_id, user_id, status, interactions_count,
    screenshots_count, started_at, completed_at
)

-- Recording interactions
recording_interactions (
    id, recording_id, type, coordinates, timestamp,
    duration, data
)
```

### 4.2 Indexing Strategy

```sql
-- For fast script lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_scripts_user_created 
ON test_scripts(created_by, created_at DESC);

-- For execution queries
CREATE INDEX idx_executions_script_created 
ON test_executions(test_script_id, created_at DESC);

-- For result queries
CREATE INDEX idx_results_execution_step 
ON test_results(execution_id, step_number);
```

---

## 5. API Design

### 5.1 REST Endpoints

```
POST /api/v1/tests/record/start
  - Body: { device_id, app_package, timeout_seconds }
  - Returns: { recording_id, status }

POST /api/v1/tests/record/{recording_id}/stop
  - Returns: { recording_id, status: "processing" }

GET /api/v1/tests/scripts
  - Query params: limit, offset, filter
  - Returns: Script list with pagination

POST /api/v1/tests/scripts
  - Body: { name, description, format, content }
  - Returns: Created script

GET /api/v1/tests/scripts/{script_id}
  - Returns: Script with full content

PUT /api/v1/tests/scripts/{script_id}
  - Body: { name, description, content }
  - Returns: Updated script

DELETE /api/v1/tests/scripts/{script_id}
  - Returns: { success: true }

POST /api/v1/tests/execute
  - Body: { script_id, device_ids, parameters, timeout }
  - Returns: { execution_id, status }

GET /api/v1/tests/executions/{execution_id}
  - Returns: Execution with results

GET /api/v1/tests/results/{execution_id}
  - Returns: Detailed results per step

POST /api/v1/tests/reports/generate
  - Body: { execution_ids, format }
  - Returns: { report_id, status }

GET /api/v1/tests/reports/{report_id}
  - Returns: Report content
```

## 8. Performance Considerations

### 8.1 Recording Performance
- Use efficient interaction capture (minimal overhead)
- Compress screenshots on-the-fly
- Batch database writes
- Cache device state

### 8.2 Execution Performance
- Parallel execution across devices
- Efficient wait condition checking
- Minimize screenshot transfers
- Use connection pooling

### 8.3 Storage Optimization
- Compress screenshots (JPEG quality 70%)
- Store only changed frames
- Archive old results
- Implement cleanup policies

---

## 9. Security Considerations

### 9.1 Authentication & Authorization
- Validate JWT tokens on all endpoints
- Check device access permissions
- Implement role-based access control
- Log all access attempts

### 9.2 Data Protection
- Encrypt sensitive data at rest
- Use HTTPS/TLS for all communications
- Implement rate limiting
- Sanitize all user inputs

### 9.3 Script Security
- Validate script content before execution
- Restrict shell commands
- Prevent script injection
- Audit script modifications

---

## 10. Monitoring & Observability

### 10.1 Prometheus Metrics
```
test_recordings_started_total
test_recordings_completed_total
test_executions_started_total
test_executions_passed_total
test_executions_failed_total
test_execution_duration_seconds
test_flaky_rate
```

### 10.2 Logging
- Log all recording operations
- Log all execution steps
- Log all failures with context
- Log performance metrics

---

## 11. Error Handling

### 11.1 Recording Errors
- Device disconnected: Pause recording, attempt resume
- Interaction capture failed: Log error, continue
- Screenshot capture failed: Skip screenshot, continue
- Storage full: Stop recording, notify user

### 11.2 Execution Errors
- Device disconnected: Fail execution, retry if configured
- Interaction failed: Capture screenshot, log error
- Assertion failed: Mark step as failed, continue or abort
- Timeout: Fail step, continue or abort

---

## 12. Testing Strategy

### 12.1 Unit Tests
- Test interaction capture
- Test script generation
- Test script validation
- Test execution engine
- Target: 80%+ coverage

### 12.2 Integration Tests
- Test end-to-end recording flow
- Test script execution
- Test result collection
- Test report generation

### 12.3 Performance Tests
- Load test with 100+ concurrent executions
- Stress test script generation
- Test storage with large result sets
- Measure latency and throughput

---

```
│  │ - PostgreSQL driver                                  │   │
│  │ - Redis client                                       │   │
│  │ - Device service client                              │   │
│  │ - Stream service client                              │   │
│  │ - WebSocket manager                                  │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Key Classes/Interfaces

```go
// Group Management
type GroupManager interface {
    CreateGroup(ctx context.Context, req *GroupRequest) (*Group, error)
    GetGroup(ctx context.Context, groupID string) (*Group, error)
    UpdateGroup(ctx context.Context, groupID string, req *GroupRequest) (*Group, error)
    DeleteGroup(ctx context.Context, groupID string) error
    ListGroups(ctx context.Context, filter *GroupFilter) ([]*Group, error)
    AddDeviceToGroup(ctx context.Context, groupID, deviceID string, role string) error
    RemoveDeviceFromGroup(ctx context.Context, groupID, deviceID string) error
}

type Group struct {
    ID          string
    Name        string
    Description string
    GroupType   string // "manual", "dynamic", "preset"
    Devices     []GroupDevice
    CreatedBy   string
    CreatedAt   time.Time
    UpdatedAt   time.Time
}

type GroupDevice struct {
    DeviceID string
    Role     string // "master", "slave"
    Status   string // "online", "offline"
    AddedAt  time.Time
}

// Session Management
type SessionManager interface {
    StartSession(ctx context.Context, req *SessionRequest) (*Session, error)
    GetSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*Session, error)
    PauseSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error
    ResumeSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error
    EndSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) error
    ListSessions(ctx context.Context, filter *SessionFilter) ([]*Session, error)
}

type Session struct {
    ID        string
    GroupID   string
    Name      string
    Status    string // "active", "paused", "completed"
    StartedAt time.Time
    EndedAt   *time.Time
    Commands  []Command
    CreatedBy string
}

// Synchronization Coordination
type SyncCoordinator interface {
    SyncInput(ctx context.Context, req *SyncInputRequest) (*SyncResult, error)
    GetSyncLatency(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (map[string]int32, error)
    HandleDeviceDisconnect(ctx context.Context, sessionID, deviceID string) error
}

type SyncInputRequest struct {
    SessionID      string
    MasterDeviceID string
    InputType      string // "tap", "swipe", "text_input", etc.
    Coordinates    [2]int32
    Data           map[string]interface{}
    SyncMode       string // "mirror", "relative"
}

type SyncResult struct {
    SuccessCount int32
    FailureCount int32
    PartialCount int32
    Latencies    map[string]int32 // device_id -> latency_ms
    Errors       map[string]string // device_id -> error_message
}

// Command Broadcasting
type CommandBroadcaster interface {
    BroadcastCommand(ctx context.Context, req *BroadcastRequest) (*BroadcastResult, error)
    GetCommandStatus(ctx context.Context, commandID string) (*CommandStatus, error)
}

type BroadcastRequest struct {
    SessionID   string
    GroupID     string
    CommandType string // "app_launch", "app_close", "restart", etc.
    CommandData map[string]interface{}
    Concurrency int32 // parallel or sequential
    Timeout     int32
}

type BroadcastResult struct {
    CommandID    string
    SuccessCount int32
    FailureCount int32
    Results      map[string]CommandResult // device_id -> result
}

type CommandResult struct {
    DeviceID  string
    Status    string // "success", "failed", "timeout"
    Output    string
    Error     string
    Duration  int32
}

// Conflict Resolution
type ConflictResolver interface {
    ResolveConflict(ctx context.Context, conflict *Conflict) (*Resolution, error)
    GetConflictHistory(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) ([]*Conflict, error)
}

type Conflict struct {
    ID          string
    SessionID   string
    Type        string // "device_disconnect", "sync_failure", "state_mismatch"
    AffectedDevices []string
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Details     map[string]interface{}
}

type Resolution struct {
    Strategy string // "abort", "retry", "skip", "manual"
    Action   string
    Result   string
}

// Latency Optimization
type LatencyOptimizer interface {
    OptimizeSync(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*OptimizationResult, error)
    GetLatencyMetrics(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*LatencyMetrics, error)
}

type LatencyMetrics struct {
    AverageLatency int32
    MaxLatency     int32
    MinLatency     int32
    P95Latency     int32
    P99Latency     int32
    Bottlenecks    []string
}
```

---

## 3. Data Flow

### 3.1 Synchronization Flow

```
User performs input on master device
    │
    ▼
Input captured by stream-service
    │
    ▼
SyncCoordinator.SyncInput()
    │
    ├─► Validate input
    │
    ├─► Map coordinates (if needed)
    │
    ├─► Broadcast to all slave devices
    │
    ├─► Monitor execution on each device
    │
    ├─► Collect latency metrics
    │
    ├─► Handle failures (retry, skip, abort)
    │
    ├─► Log sync operation
    │
    ▼
Return sync result to UI
    │
    ├─► Success count
    │
    ├─► Failure count
    │
    ├─► Latency per device
    │
    ▼
Update UI with sync status
```

### 3.2 Command Broadcast Flow

```
User selects broadcast command
    │
    ▼
CommandBroadcaster.BroadcastCommand()
    │
    ├─► Create command record
    │
    ├─► Queue command for each device
    │
    ├─► Execute in parallel or sequential
    │
    ├─► Monitor execution
    │
    ├─► Collect results
    │
    ├─► Handle failures (retry, skip)
    │
    ├─► Log command execution
    │
    ▼
Return broadcast result
    │
    ├─► Success count
    │
    ├─► Failure count
    │
    ├─► Results per device
    │
    ▼
Update UI with command status
```

### 3.3 Session Recording Flow

```
Session starts
    │
    ├─► Record all inputs
    │
    ├─► Record all commands
    │
    ├─► Record device states
    │
    ├─► Record performance metrics
    │
    ├─► Record errors/conflicts
    │
    ▼
Session ends
    │
    ├─► Finalize session recording
    │
    ├─► Store in database
    │
    ├─► Generate session summary
    │
    ▼
Session available for replay
```

---

## 4. Database Design

### 4.1 Schema Overview

```sql
-- Device groups
device_groups (
    id, name, description, group_type,
    created_by, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
)

-- Group devices
group_devices (
    id, group_id, device_id, role, added_at
)

-- Sync sessions
sync_sessions (
    id, group_id, name, started_at, ended_at,
    duration_seconds, status, created_by
)

-- Sync commands
sync_commands (
    id, session_id, command_type, command_data,
    executed_at, status
)

-- Sync operations (for latency tracking)
sync_operations (
    id, session_id, device_id, operation_type,
    latency_ms, status, timestamp
)
```

### 4.2 Indexing Strategy

```sql
-- For fast group lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_groups_user_created 
ON device_groups(created_by, created_at DESC);

-- For session queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_group_created 
ON sync_sessions(group_id, created_at DESC);

-- For command queries
CREATE INDEX idx_commands_session_created 
ON sync_commands(session_id, executed_at DESC);

-- For latency analysis
CREATE INDEX idx_operations_session_device 
ON sync_operations(session_id, device_id);
```

---

## 5. API Design

### 5.1 REST Endpoints

```
POST /api/v1/sync/groups
  - Body: { name, description, group_type }
  - Returns: Created group

GET /api/v1/sync/groups
  - Query params: limit, offset, filter
  - Returns: Group list

GET /api/v1/sync/groups/{group_id}
  - Returns: Group with devices

PUT /api/v1/sync/groups/{group_id}
  - Body: { name, description }
  - Returns: Updated group

DELETE /api/v1/sync/groups/{group_id}
  - Returns: { success: true }

POST /api/v1/sync/groups/{group_id}/devices
  - Body: { device_id, role }
  - Returns: Updated group

DELETE /api/v1/sync/groups/{group_id}/devices/{device_id}
  - Returns: Updated group

POST /api/v1/sync/sessions/start
  - Body: { group_id, name }
  - Returns: { session_id, status }

POST /api/v1/sync/sessions/{session_id}/pause
  - Returns: Updated session

POST /api/v1/sync/sessions/{session_id}/resume
  - Returns: Updated session

POST /api/v1/sync/sessions/{session_id}/stop
  - Returns: Updated session

POST /api/v1/sync/commands/broadcast
  - Body: { session_id, command_type, command_data }
  - Returns: { command_id, status }

GET /api/v1/sync/sessions
  - Query params: limit, offset, filter
  - Returns: Session list

GET /api/v1/sync/sessions/{session_id}
  - Returns: Session with commands
```

### 5.2 WebSocket API

```
WS /ws/sync/sessions/{session_id}
  - Subscribe to session updates
  - Message: { type: "input_sync", result: {...} }
  - Message: { type: "command_broadcast", result: {...} }
  - Message: { type: "device_status", device_id, status }
  - Message: { type: "conflict", conflict: {...} }
```

---

## 6. Technology Stack

### 6.1 Backend
- **Language:** Go 1.21+
- **Framework:** Gin (REST API) + gRPC
- **Database:** PostgreSQL 14+
- **Cache:** Redis 6+
- **Message Queue:** Redis or NATS
- **Monitoring:** Prometheus + Grafana

### 6.2 Frontend
- **Framework:** React 18+
- **State Management:** Redux or Zustand
- **Grid Layout:** React Grid Layout
- **UI Components:** Material-UI or custom

### 6.3 Infrastructure
- **Containerization:** Docker
- **Orchestration:** Kubernetes (optional)
- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions or GitLab CI

---

## 7. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Group Management (Week 1-2)
- [ ] Set up sync service project
- [ ] Implement group CRUD operations
- [ ] Implement device management
- [ ] Create database schema

### Phase 2: Session Management (Week 2-3)

### Phase 3: Input Synchronization (Week 3-4)

- [ ] Implement coordinate mapping

- [ ] Add latency monitoring

### Phase 4: Command Broadcasting (Week 4-5)
- [ ] Implement command execution
- [ ] Implement parallel/sequential execution
- [ ] Implement result collection
- [ ] Add command UI

### Phase 5: Multi-device View (Week 5-6)
- [ ] Implement grid layout
- [ ] Implement synchronized screen display
- [ ] Implement comparison view
- [ ] Add responsive design

### Phase 6: Testing & Deployment (Week 6-7)
- [ ] End-to-end testing
- [ ] Performance testing
- [ ] Security testing
- [ ] Deployment

---

## 8. Performance Considerations

### 8.1 Synchronization Performance
- Use efficient input capture (minimal overhead)
- Batch commands for efficiency
- Parallel execution where possible
- Network optimization (compression, caching)

### 8.2 Latency Optimization
- Monitor latency per device
- Identify bottlenecks
- Adjust sync parameters
- Use adaptive timeouts

### 8.3 Scalability
- Support 50+ concurrent devices
- Efficient database queries
- Connection pooling
- Message queue for command distribution

---

## 9. Security Considerations

### 9.1 Authentication & Authorization
- Validate JWT tokens on all endpoints
- Check device access permissions
- Implement role-based access control
- Log all access attempts

### 9.2 Command Security
- Restrict broadcast commands to safe operations
- Validate command parameters
- Prevent command injection
- Audit all commands

### 9.3 Data Protection
- Encrypt session recordings at rest
- Use HTTPS/TLS for all communications
- Implement rate limiting
- Sanitize all user inputs

---

## 10. Monitoring & Observability

### 10.1 Prometheus Metrics
```
sync_sessions_started_total
sync_sessions_completed_total
sync_input_latency_ms
sync_command_broadcast_total
sync_command_success_rate
sync_conflicts_total
```

### 10.2 Logging
- Log all group operations
- Log all session events
- Log all sync operations
- Log all conflicts

---

## 11. Error Handling

### 11.1 Sync Errors
- Device disconnected: Pause sync, attempt resume
- Sync failed: Retry or skip device
- Coordinate mapping failed: Use fallback
- Timeout: Abort or retry

### 11.2 Command Errors
- Command failed: Retry or skip device
- Timeout: Abort or retry
- Invalid command: Reject with error
- Permission denied: Log and notify

---

## 12. Testing Strategy

### 12.1 Unit Tests
- Test group management
- Test session management
- Test coordinate mapping
- Test command execution
- Target: 80%+ coverage

### 12.2 Integration Tests
- Test end-to-end sync flow
- Test command broadcasting
- Test session recording/replay
- Test conflict resolution

### 12.3 Performance Tests
- Load test with 50+ concurrent devices
- Stress test command broadcasting
- Test latency under load
- Measure throughput

---

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|

# Implementation Tasks: Multi-device Sync Control

**Feature ID:** MDC-001  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-16  
**Status:** Ready for Implementation

---

## Overview

This document breaks down the Multi-device Sync Control feature into actionable implementation tasks organized by phase and component.

**Total Estimated Effort:** 5-6 weeks  
**Team Size:** 2-3 developers  
**Priority:** High

---

## Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (Week 1-2)

### Task MDC-1.1: Project Setup and Infrastructure
**Estimated:** 2 days  
**Owner:** DevOps/Backend Lead

**Description:**
Set up the sync service project structure, dependencies, and infrastructure.

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Create Go project structure following company standards
- [ ] Set up module dependencies (Gin, gRPC, PostgreSQL driver, Redis client)
- [ ] Create Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml
- [ ] Set up PostgreSQL instance
- [ ] Set up Redis instance
- [ ] Configure environment variables and config management
- [ ] Set up logging infrastructure (zap/logrus)
- [ ] Set up Prometheus metrics exporter
- [ ] Create CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions)
- [ ] Set up code linting and formatting (golangci-lint)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Project builds successfully
- [ ] Docker containers start without errors
- [ ] All dependencies installed and working
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline runs on every commit
- [ ] Logging and metrics working

**Dependencies:** None

---

### Task MDC-1.2: Database Schema and Migrations
**Estimated:** 2 days  
**Owner:** Backend Developer

**Description:**
Create PostgreSQL schema for device groups, sessions, and commands.

**Subtasks:**

- [ ] Create group_devices table
- [ ] Create sync_sessions table
- [ ] Create sync_commands table
- [ ] Create sync_operations table (for latency tracking)
- [ ] Create indexes for common queries
- [ ] Create migration scripts (golang-migrate)
- [ ] Add seed data for testing
- [ ] Document schema design
- [ ] Test migration rollback

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Indexes created and optimized

### Task MDC-5.3: Stream Service Integration
**Estimated:** 1 day

Performance testing and optimization.

### Task MDC-5.6: Security Testing

Security testing and vulnerability assessment.

## Phase 6: Documentation and Deployment (Week 5-6)

### Task MDC-6.2: Deployment and DevOps

Prepare for production deployment.
