input.text:
# Feature Requirements: AI-powered Object Detection

**Feature ID:** AOD-001  
**Priority:** Medium  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-17  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
AI-powered object detection system that automatically identifies and classifies UI elements on Android device screens, generates element locators, and integrates with test automation framework for intelligent test creation and maintenance.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Reduce manual effort in identifying UI elements for testing
- Enable automatic test script generation with element locators
- Improve test maintenance by detecting UI changes
- Support visual regression testing
- Enable accessibility testing through element detection
- Reduce test creation time by 70%

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- Object detection accuracy > 95%
- Element classification accuracy > 90%
- Detection latency < 2 seconds per screen
- Support 100+ concurrent detection requests
- Locator generation success rate > 95%

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 Object Detection

#### 2.1.1 UI Element Detection
**REQ-AOD-001:** The system SHALL detect the following UI elements:
- Buttons (standard, icon, floating action buttons)
- Text fields (input, password, search)
- Labels and text views
- Images and icons
- Checkboxes and radio buttons
- Switches and toggles
- Dropdowns and spinners
- Lists and recycler views
- Tabs and navigation bars
- Dialogs and popups
- Cards and containers

**REQ-AOD-002:** For each detected element, the system SHALL capture:
- Element type/class
- Bounding box coordinates (x, y, width, height)
- Confidence score (0-100%)
- Visual properties (color, size, shape)
- Text content (if applicable)
- Icon/image content (if applicable)
- Hierarchy level
- Parent/child relationships

**REQ-AOD-003:** The system SHALL support detection from:
- Live device screens (real-time)
- Screenshots (static images)
- Screen recordings (video frames)
- Test execution screenshots

**REQ-AOD-004:** Detection SHALL work across:
- Different Android versions (5.0+)
- Different screen resolutions
- Different device orientations (portrait, landscape)
- Light and dark themes
- Custom UI frameworks (Material Design, custom themes)

#### 2.1.2 Detection Modes
**REQ-AOD-005:** The system SHALL support multiple detection modes:
- **Full screen:** Detect all elements on screen
- **Region of interest:** Detect elements in specific area
- **Element type filter:** Detect only specific element types
- **Confidence threshold:** Filter by minimum confidence score

**REQ-AOD-006:** Users SHALL be able to configure detection parameters:
- Minimum confidence threshold (default 70%)
- Maximum elements to detect (default 100)
- Element types to include/exclude
- Detection timeout (default 10 seconds)

#### 2.1.3 Model Training and Updates
**REQ-AOD-007:** The system SHALL use pre-trained ML models:
- Base model trained on common Android UI patterns
- Support for custom model training (future)
- Model versioning and updates
- A/B testing for model improvements

**REQ-AOD-008:** The system SHALL support model updates:
- Hot-swap models without service restart
- Rollback to previous model version
- Model performance monitoring
- Automatic model selection based on device type

### 2.2 Element Classification

#### 2.2.1 Element Type Classification
**REQ-AOD-009:** The system SHALL classify detected elements into categories:
- **Interactive:** Buttons, text fields, checkboxes, switches
- **Display:** Labels, images, icons
- **Container:** Lists, cards, dialogs, layouts
- **Navigation:** Tabs, menus, navigation bars
- **Custom:** User-defined element types

**REQ-AOD-010:** Classification SHALL include:
- Primary type (button, text field, etc.)
- Sub-type (icon button, password field, etc.)
- State (enabled, disabled, selected, focused)
- Attributes (clickable, editable, scrollable)

**REQ-AOD-011:** The system SHALL detect element states:
- Enabled/disabled
- Selected/unselected
- Focused/unfocused
- Visible/hidden
- Checked/unchecked (for checkboxes)

#### 2.2.2 Text Recognition (OCR)
**REQ-AOD-012:** The system SHALL extract text from detected elements:
- Button labels
- Text field content
- Labels and descriptions
- Error messages
- Toast notifications

**REQ-AOD-013:** Text recognition SHALL support:
- Multiple languages (English, Indonesian, etc.)
- Different fonts and sizes
- Text on images
- Rotated text
- Partial text (truncated)

#### 2.2.3 Icon Recognition
**REQ-AOD-014:** The system SHALL recognize common icons:
- Material Design icons
- Font Awesome icons
- Custom app icons
- System icons

**REQ-AOD-015:** Icon recognition SHALL provide:
- Icon name/identifier
- Icon category (navigation, action, content, etc.)
- Confidence score
- Similar icon suggestions

### 2.3 Locator Generation

#### 2.3.1 Locator Strategies
**REQ-AOD-016:** The system SHALL generate multiple locator strategies:
- **Resource ID:** android:id/element_id
- **Text:** Exact text match
- **Content description:** Accessibility label
- **XPath:** XML path to element
- **Class name:** Element class
- **Coordinates:** Absolute position (fallback)
- **Image matching:** Visual similarity (fallback)

**REQ-AOD-017:** Locator generation SHALL prioritize:
1. Resource ID (most stable)
2. Content description
3. Text content
4. XPath
5. Class name
6. Coordinates (least stable)

**REQ-AOD-018:** The system SHALL generate robust locators:
- Unique within screen context
- Resilient to minor UI changes
- Human-readable
- Compatible with Appium/UIAutomator

**REQ-AOD-019:** For elements without stable locators, the system SHALL:
- Generate relative locators (near, above, below, left, right)
- Use parent-child relationships
- Combine multiple attributes
- Suggest manual locator improvement

#### 2.3.2 Locator Validation
**REQ-AOD-020:** The system SHALL validate generated locators:
- Check uniqueness on current screen
- Verify element can be found using locator
- Test locator stability across screens
- Suggest improvements for weak locators

**REQ-AOD-021:** Locator validation SHALL provide:
- Uniqueness score (0-100%)
- Stability score (0-100%)
- Recommended locator strategy
- Alternative locators

### 2.4 Integration with Test Automation

#### 2.4.1 Test Script Enhancement
**REQ-AOD-022:** The system SHALL integrate with test recording:
- Automatically detect elements during recording
- Generate locators for recorded interactions
- Replace coordinate-based interactions with element-based
- Suggest assertions based on detected elements

**REQ-AOD-023:** The system SHALL enhance test scripts:
- Add element locators to existing scripts
- Replace fragile locators with robust ones
- Add element validation steps
- Generate page object models

**REQ-AOD-024:** The system SHALL support test maintenance:
- Detect when UI elements change
- Suggest locator updates
- Identify broken locators
- Auto-fix simple locator issues

#### 2.4.2 Visual Regression Testing
**REQ-AOD-025:** The system SHALL support visual regression testing:
- Compare element positions across test runs
- Detect layout changes
- Identify missing/new elements
- Generate visual diff reports

**REQ-AOD-026:** Visual regression SHALL detect:
- Element position changes
- Element size changes
- Element appearance changes (color, style)
- Missing elements
- New elements

#### 2.4.3 Accessibility Testing
**REQ-AOD-027:** The system SHALL support accessibility testing:
- Detect elements without content descriptions
- Identify insufficient touch target sizes
- Check color contrast ratios
- Validate text readability
- Detect missing labels

**REQ-AOD-028:** Accessibility reports SHALL include:
- List of accessibility issues
- Severity levels (critical, warning, info)
- Suggested fixes
- WCAG compliance status

### 2.5 Element Interaction

#### 2.5.1 Element Highlighting
**REQ-AOD-029:** The system SHALL provide element highlighting:
- Overlay bounding boxes on detected elements
- Color-code by element type
- Display element properties on hover
- Show confidence scores

**REQ-AOD-030:** Highlighting SHALL be configurable:
- Show/hide specific element types
- Adjust transparency
- Toggle labels and properties
- Export highlighted screenshots

#### 2.5.2 Element Selection
**REQ-AOD-031:** Users SHALL be able to select detected elements:
- Click on element in UI
- Select from element list
- Search by properties
- Filter by type or confidence

**REQ-AOD-032:** Selected elements SHALL display:
- All detected properties
- Generated locators
- Hierarchy information
- Similar elements

### 2.6 Performance Optimization

#### 2.6.1 Detection Optimization
**REQ-AOD-033:** The system SHALL optimize detection performance:
- Cache detection results (5-minute TTL)
- Batch multiple detection requests
- Use GPU acceleration (if available)
- Implement progressive detection (quick scan + detailed scan)

**REQ-AOD-034:** The system SHALL support incremental detection:
- Detect only changed regions
- Reuse previous detection results
- Skip unchanged screens

#### 2.6.2 Model Optimization
**REQ-AOD-035:** The system SHALL optimize model performance:
- Use quantized models for faster inference
- Support multiple model sizes (small, medium, large)
- Automatic model selection based on device capabilities
- Edge deployment for low-latency detection

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-AOD-NFR-001:** Object detection SHALL complete within 2 seconds per screen.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-002:** The system SHALL support 100+ concurrent detection requests.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-003:** Detection accuracy SHALL be > 95% for common UI elements.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-004:** Classification accuracy SHALL be > 90%.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-005:** Locator generation SHALL complete within 1 second.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-AOD-NFR-006:** The system SHALL scale to support 1000+ devices.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-007:** Model inference SHALL support batch processing (up to 10 images).

**REQ-AOD-NFR-008:** Detection results SHALL be cached for 5 minutes.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-AOD-NFR-009:** Detection service SHALL have 99.5% uptime.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-010:** Failed detections SHALL be automatically retried up to 3 times.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-011:** Model updates SHALL not cause service downtime.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-012:** Detection results SHALL be persisted for 30 days.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-AOD-NFR-013:** All detection APIs SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-014:** Screenshots SHALL be encrypted at rest.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-015:** Model files SHALL be integrity-checked before loading.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-016:** Access to detection results SHALL be logged for audit.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-AOD-NFR-017:** Detection service SHALL be implemented as separate microservice in Python.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-018:** Codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-019:** System SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-AOD-NFR-020:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Test Service Integration
**REQ-AOD-INT-001:** Detection service SHALL integrate with test-service for test enhancement.

**REQ-AOD-INT-002:** Test-service SHALL provide screenshots for detection.

### 4.2 Recording Service Integration
**REQ-AOD-INT-003:** Detection service SHALL integrate with recording-service for real-time detection.

**REQ-AOD-INT-004:** Recording-service SHALL provide video frames for detection.

### 4.3 Device Service Integration
**REQ-AOD-INT-005:** Detection service SHALL integrate with device-service for live screen capture.

### 4.4 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-AOD-INT-006:** All detection APIs SHALL be exposed through api-gateway.

**REQ-AOD-INT-007:** API-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting for detection endpoints.

### 4.5 Storage Integration
**REQ-AOD-INT-008:** Detection service SHALL store results in PostgreSQL.

**REQ-AOD-INT-009:** Detection service SHALL cache results in Redis.

**REQ-AOD-INT-010:** Detection service SHALL store screenshots in S3/MinIO.

---

## 5. User Interface Requirements

### 5.1 Detection UI
**REQ-AOD-UI-001:** Detection interface SHALL display:
- Screenshot with element overlays
- List of detected elements
- Element properties panel
- Locator generation panel

### 5.2 Element Inspector
**REQ-AOD-UI-002:** Element inspector SHALL display:
- Element hierarchy tree
- Element properties
- Generated locators
- Similar elements

### 5.3 Visual Regression UI
**REQ-AOD-UI-003:** Visual regression interface SHALL display:
- Side-by-side comparison
- Highlighted differences
- Element change summary
- Regression report

---

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 detection_results Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE detection_results (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    screenshot_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    device_id UUID REFERENCES devices(id),
    model_version VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    detection_time_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
    elements_detected INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.2 detected_elements Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE detected_elements (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    detection_result_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES detection_results(id),
    element_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    bounding_box JSONB NOT NULL,
    confidence_score NUMERIC(5, 2) NOT NULL,
    properties JSONB,
    locators JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

---

## 7. API Requirements

### 7.1 REST API Endpoints

```
POST /api/v1/detection/detect
  - Body: { screenshot_url, device_id, options }
  - Returns: { detection_id, elements }

GET /api/v1/detection/results/{detection_id}
  - Returns: Detection result with elements

POST /api/v1/detection/generate-locators
  - Body: { element_id }
  - Returns: { locators }

POST /api/v1/detection/visual-regression
  - Body: { baseline_screenshot, current_screenshot }
  - Returns: { differences, report }

GET /api/v1/detection/models
  - Returns: Available models

POST /api/v1/detection/models/{model_id}/activate
  - Returns: { success: true }
```

---

## 8. ML Model Requirements

### 8.1 Model Architecture
**REQ-AOD-ML-001:** The system SHALL use object detection models:
- YOLO (You Only Look Once) for real-time detection
- Faster R-CNN for high-accuracy detection
- MobileNet for edge deployment

**REQ-AOD-ML-002:** Models SHALL be trained on:
- Android UI screenshots (100,000+ images)
- Multiple Android versions
- Various screen resolutions
- Different themes and styles

### 8.2 Model Performance
**REQ-AOD-ML-003:** Models SHALL achieve:
- mAP (mean Average Precision) > 0.90
- Inference time < 500ms per image
- Model size < 100MB (for edge deployment)

---

## 9. Acceptance Criteria

### 9.1 Feature Complete Criteria
- [ ] All functional requirements implemented
- [ ] All non-functional requirements met
- [ ] ML models trained and deployed
- [ ] UI components implemented
- [ ] Database schema deployed
- [ ] API documentation completed
- [ ] Unit tests achieve 80%+ coverage
- [ ] Integration tests cover critical flows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met

### 9.2 User Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can detect elements from screenshots
- [ ] Detection accuracy > 95%
- [ ] Locators generated successfully
- [ ] Integration with test automation working
- [ ] Visual regression detection working
- [ ] Detection completes within 2 seconds

---

## 10. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-05-17 | Engineering Team | Initial requirements document |

input.text:
# Feature Requirements: Remote File Manager

**Feature ID:** RFM-001  
**Priority:** Medium  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-17  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
Remote file management system that enables users to browse, upload, download, and manage files on Android devices through a web-based interface, supporting batch operations, file type filtering, and integration with test automation workflows.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Enable easy file transfer between desktop and devices
- Support test data management (APKs, test files, media)
- Facilitate debugging by accessing device logs and files
- Enable bulk file operations across multiple devices
- Reduce manual file management effort by 80%
- Support automated test data setup

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- File transfer speed > 10 MB/s
- Support 50+ concurrent file operations
- File browser response time < 1 second
- Upload/download success rate > 99%
- Support files up to 2GB

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 File Browsing

#### 2.1.1 Directory Navigation
**REQ-RFM-001:** Users SHALL be able to browse device file system:
- Internal storage (/sdcard/)
- External storage (SD card)
- App-specific directories (/data/data/)
- System directories (with root access)
- Download directory
- Pictures/Videos/Music directories

**REQ-RFM-002:** File browser SHALL display:
- File/folder name
- File size
- File type/extension
- Last modified date
- Permissions (read, write, execute)
- File icon based on type

**REQ-RFM-003:** Navigation SHALL support:
- Click to enter directory
- Breadcrumb navigation
- Back/forward navigation
- Jump to path (type path directly)
- Bookmarks for frequent directories
- Recent directories history

**REQ-RFM-004:** The system SHALL support multiple view modes:
- List view (detailed information)
- Grid view (thumbnails for images/videos)
- Tree view (hierarchical structure)

#### 2.1.2 File Search
**REQ-RFM-005:** Users SHALL be able to search for files:
- By file name (exact match, partial match, wildcard)
- By file type/extension
- By file size (range)
- By date modified (range)
- By content (text files only)

**REQ-RFM-006:** Search SHALL support:
- Recursive search in subdirectories
- Case-sensitive/insensitive search
- Regular expression search
- Save search queries
- Search history

**REQ-RFM-007:** Search results SHALL display:
- Matching files with full path
- File properties
- Highlight matching text (for content search)
- Sort and filter results

#### 2.1.3 File Filtering
**REQ-RFM-008:** Users SHALL be able to filter files by:
- File type (images, videos, audio, documents, APKs, etc.)
- File size (< 1MB, 1-10MB, 10-100MB, > 100MB)
- Date modified (today, this week, this month, custom range)
- Hidden files (show/hide)

**REQ-RFM-009:** The system SHALL provide quick filters:
- Images (jpg, png, gif, etc.)
- Videos (mp4, avi, mkv, etc.)
- Audio (mp3, wav, etc.)
- Documents (pdf, doc, txt, etc.)
- APKs
- Logs (log, txt)

### 2.2 File Upload

#### 2.2.1 Upload Methods
**REQ-RFM-010:** Users SHALL be able to upload files via:
- Drag and drop
- File picker dialog
- Paste from clipboard (images)
- URL download (download from URL to device)

**REQ-RFM-011:** Upload SHALL support:
- Single file upload
- Multiple file upload (batch)
- Folder upload (with structure preservation)
- Resume interrupted uploads

**REQ-RFM-012:** Upload destination SHALL be:
- User-selected directory
- Default download directory
- App-specific directory
- Custom path

#### 2.2.2 Upload Progress
**REQ-RFM-013:** The system SHALL display upload progress:
- Overall progress (percentage, MB transferred)
- Per-file progress (for batch uploads)
- Transfer speed (MB/s)
- Estimated time remaining
- Pause/resume/cancel buttons

**REQ-RFM-014:** Upload SHALL handle errors:
- Insufficient storage space
- Permission denied
- Network interruption
- File already exists (overwrite, skip, rename)

#### 2.2.3 Upload Validation
**REQ-RFM-015:** The system SHALL validate uploads:
- Check available storage space before upload
- Verify file integrity after upload (checksum)
- Validate file type (if restrictions apply)
- Check file size limits

**REQ-RFM-016:** Upload restrictions SHALL be configurable:
- Maximum file size (default 2GB)
- Allowed file types
- Maximum concurrent uploads (default 5)
- Upload rate limiting

### 2.3 File Download

#### 2.3.1 Download Methods
**REQ-RFM-017:** Users SHALL be able to download files:
- Single file download
- Multiple file download (as ZIP)
- Folder download (as ZIP with structure)
- Download to custom location on desktop

**REQ-RFM-018:** Download SHALL support:
- Resume interrupted downloads
- Parallel downloads (multiple files)
- Background downloads
- Download queue

#### 2.3.2 Download Progress
**REQ-RFM-019:** The system SHALL display download progress:
- Overall progress (percentage, MB transferred)
- Per-file progress (for batch downloads)
- Transfer speed (MB/s)
- Estimated time remaining
- Pause/resume/cancel buttons

**REQ-RFM-020:** Download SHALL handle errors:
- File not found
- Permission denied
- Network interruption
- Disk full on desktop

#### 2.3.3 Download Optimization
**REQ-RFM-021:** The system SHALL optimize downloads:
- Compress files before transfer (optional)
- Use chunked transfer for large files
- Cache frequently downloaded files
- Parallel chunk downloads

### 2.4 File Operations

#### 2.4.1 Basic Operations
**REQ-RFM-022:** Users SHALL be able to perform file operations:
- Copy file/folder
- Move file/folder
- Rename file/folder
- Delete file/folder
- Create new folder
- Create new file (empty)

**REQ-RFM-023:** File operations SHALL support:
- Batch operations (multiple files)
- Undo/redo (for reversible operations)
- Confirmation dialogs (for destructive operations)
- Progress tracking (for long operations)

**REQ-RFM-024:** Delete operations SHALL:
- Move to trash/recycle bin (soft delete)
- Permanent delete (with confirmation)
- Bulk delete with confirmation
- Delete empty folders

#### 2.4.2 Advanced Operations
**REQ-RFM-025:** Users SHALL be able to perform advanced operations:
- Compress files/folders (ZIP, TAR, GZ)
- Extract archives (ZIP, TAR, GZ, RAR)
- Change file permissions (chmod)
- Change file ownership (chown, requires root)
- Create symbolic links (requires root)

**REQ-RFM-026:** The system SHALL support file comparison:
- Compare two files (text diff)
- Compare file checksums (MD5, SHA256)
- Compare file sizes and dates

#### 2.4.3 File Preview
**REQ-RFM-027:** The system SHALL provide file preview:
- Text files (txt, log, json, xml, etc.)
- Images (jpg, png, gif, etc.)
- Videos (mp4, avi, etc.) - thumbnail or player
- Audio (mp3, wav, etc.) - player
- PDF documents
- APK information (package name, version, permissions)

**REQ-RFM-028:** Preview SHALL support:
- Syntax highlighting (for code files)
- Line numbers (for text files)
- Search within file
- Download from preview
- Open in external editor

### 2.5 Multi-device Operations

#### 2.5.1 Device Selection
**REQ-RFM-029:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Select multiple devices
- Perform operations on all selected devices
- View file browser for each device side-by-side
- Sync files across devices

**REQ-RFM-030:** Multi-device view SHALL display:
- File browser for each device
- Device status (online/offline)
- Storage usage per device
- Operation progress per device

#### 2.5.2 File Synchronization
**REQ-RFM-031:** Users SHALL be able to sync files:
- From desktop to multiple devices
- From one device to multiple devices
- Bidirectional sync (keep files in sync)
- Selective sync (specific folders)

**REQ-RFM-032:** Sync SHALL support:
- Conflict resolution (newer file wins, manual selection)
- Incremental sync (only changed files)
- Scheduled sync (automatic at intervals)
- Sync profiles (save sync configurations)

#### 2.5.3 Bulk Operations
**REQ-RFM-033:** Users SHALL be able to perform bulk operations:
- Upload same file to multiple devices
- Download same file from multiple devices
- Delete same file from multiple devices
- Execute same operation on multiple devices

**REQ-RFM-034:** Bulk operations SHALL:
- Execute in parallel (for speed)
- Report success/failure per device
- Continue on failure (don't abort all)
- Provide summary report

### 2.6 Storage Management

#### 2.6.1 Storage Information
**REQ-RFM-035:** The system SHALL display storage information:
- Total storage capacity
- Used storage
- Available storage
- Storage breakdown by type (apps, media, documents, etc.)
- Largest files/folders

**REQ-RFM-036:** Storage visualization SHALL include:
- Pie chart of storage usage
- Bar chart of folder sizes
- Treemap of file system
- Storage trend over time

#### 2.6.2 Storage Cleanup
**REQ-RFM-037:** The system SHALL provide cleanup tools:
- Find and delete duplicate files
- Find and delete large files
- Find and delete old files (not accessed in X days)
- Clear app caches
- Clear download folder

**REQ-RFM-038:** Cleanup SHALL:
- Preview files to be deleted
- Require confirmation before deletion
- Provide undo option (if possible)
- Report space freed

### 2.7 Integration with Test Automation

#### 2.7.1 Test Data Management
**REQ-RFM-039:** The system SHALL support test data management:
- Upload test APKs to devices
- Upload test data files (JSON, CSV, images, etc.)
- Download test results and logs
- Clean up test data after execution

**REQ-RFM-040:** Test data operations SHALL be scriptable:
- API for file upload/download
- Batch operations via API
- Integration with test scripts
- Automatic cleanup after tests

#### 2.7.2 Log Collection
**REQ-RFM-041:** The system SHALL support log collection:
- Download logcat logs
- Download app-specific logs
- Download crash logs
- Download ANR traces

**REQ-RFM-042:** Log collection SHALL:
- Filter logs by time range
- Filter logs by tag/priority
- Compress logs before download
- Automatic log collection on test failure

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-RFM-NFR-001:** File transfer speed SHALL be > 10 MB/s.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-002:** The system SHALL support 50+ concurrent file operations.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-003:** File browser SHALL respond within 1 second.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-004:** File search SHALL complete within 5 seconds for 10,000+ files.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-005:** File preview SHALL load within 2 seconds.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-RFM-NFR-006:** The system SHALL scale to support 1000+ devices.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-007:** File operations SHALL support files up to 2GB.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-008:** Bulk operations SHALL support 100+ files.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-RFM-NFR-009:** File transfer success rate SHALL be > 99%.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-010:** Failed operations SHALL be automatically retried up to 3 times.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-011:** File integrity SHALL be verified via checksums.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-012:** Interrupted transfers SHALL be resumable.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-RFM-NFR-013:** All file APIs SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-014:** File transfers SHALL use HTTPS/TLS encryption.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-015:** File access SHALL respect device permissions.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-016:** All file operations SHALL be logged for audit.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-RFM-NFR-017:** File service SHALL be implemented as separate microservice in Go.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-018:** Codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-019:** System SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-RFM-NFR-020:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Device Service Integration
**REQ-RFM-INT-001:** File service SHALL integrate with device-service for device management.

**REQ-RFM-INT-002:** Device-service SHALL provide device connection status.

### 4.2 Test Service Integration
**REQ-RFM-INT-003:** File service SHALL integrate with test-service for test data management.

**REQ-RFM-INT-004:** Test-service SHALL trigger file operations via API.

### 4.3 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-RFM-INT-005:** All file APIs SHALL be exposed through api-gateway.

**REQ-RFM-INT-006:** API-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting for file endpoints.

### 4.4 Storage Integration
**REQ-RFM-INT-007:** File service SHALL use temporary storage for file transfers.

**REQ-RFM-INT-008:** Temporary files SHALL be cleaned up after 24 hours.

---

## 5. User Interface Requirements

### 5.1 File Browser UI
**REQ-RFM-UI-001:** File browser SHALL display:
- Directory tree (left panel)
- File list (main panel)
- File properties (right panel)
- Toolbar with actions

### 5.2 Upload/Download UI
**REQ-RFM-UI-002:** Upload/download interface SHALL display:
- Drag-and-drop zone
- Progress bars
- Transfer queue
- Transfer history

### 5.3 Multi-device UI
**REQ-RFM-UI-003:** Multi-device interface SHALL display:
- Device selector
- Split view for multiple devices
- Sync controls
- Bulk operation controls

---

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 file_operations Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE file_operations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    operation_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'upload', 'download', 'delete', etc.
    source_path TEXT,
    destination_path TEXT,
    file_size BIGINT,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'pending', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'failed'
    progress INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    error_message TEXT,
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

#### 6.1.2 file_transfers Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE file_transfers (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    operation_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES file_operations(id),
    chunk_number INTEGER,
    chunk_size BIGINT,
    checksum VARCHAR(64),
    transferred_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

---

## 7. API Requirements

### 7.1 REST API Endpoints

```
GET /api/v1/files/browse
  - Query params: device_id, path
  - Returns: File list

POST /api/v1/files/upload
  - Body: multipart/form-data
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

GET /api/v1/files/download
  - Query params: device_id, path
  - Returns: File stream

POST /api/v1/files/delete
  - Body: { device_id, paths[] }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

POST /api/v1/files/copy
  - Body: { device_id, source, destination }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

POST /api/v1/files/move
  - Body: { device_id, source, destination }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

GET /api/v1/files/search
  - Query params: device_id, query, filters
  - Returns: Search results

GET /api/v1/files/storage
  - Query params: device_id
  - Returns: Storage information

POST /api/v1/files/sync
  - Body: { source_device_id, target_device_ids[], paths[] }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

GET /api/v1/files/operations/{operation_id}
  - Returns: Operation status and progress
```

---

## 8. Acceptance Criteria

### 8.1 Feature Complete Criteria
- [ ] All functional requirements implemented
- [ ] All non-functional requirements met
- [ ] UI components implemented
- [ ] Database schema deployed
- [ ] API documentation completed
- [ ] Unit tests achieve 80%+ coverage
- [ ] Integration tests cover critical flows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met

### 8.2 User Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can browse device file system
- [ ] User can upload/download files
- [ ] File transfer speed > 10 MB/s
- [ ] User can perform file operations
- [ ] Multi-device operations working
- [ ] Storage management working
- [ ] Integration with test automation working

---

## 9. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-05-17 | Engineering Team | Initial requirements document |

# Design Document: AI-powered Object Detection

**Feature ID:** AOD-001  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-17  
**Status:** Draft

---

## 1. Architecture Overview

### 1.1 System Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Desktop App (Tauri)                       │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  React UI Components                                     │   │
│  │  - Element Inspector                                     │   │
│  │  - Detection Viewer                                      │   │
│  │  - Locator Generator                                     │   │
│  │  - Visual Regression                                     │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ▲
                              │ HTTPS/WebSocket
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      API Gateway                                 │
│  - Authentication (JWT validation)                              │
│  - Rate limiting                                                │
│  - Request routing                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ▲
        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
        ▼                     ▼                     ▼
┌────────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐
│ Test           │    │ Recording      │    │ Detection      │
│ Service        │    │ Service        │    │ Service        │
│ (Go)           │    │ (Go)           │    │ (Python)       │
└────────────────┘    └────────────────┘    └────────────────┘
        ▲                     ▲                     ▲
        └─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘
                              ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  PostgreSQL + Redis + S3/MinIO          │
        │  - Detection results                    │
        │  - Detected elements                    │
        │  - ML models                            │
        │  - Screenshots                          │
        └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.2 Component Responsibilities

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|-----------------|
| **Desktop App** | Display detection results, element inspector, locator generator |
| **API Gateway** | Route requests, validate auth, rate limiting |
| **Detection Service** | ML inference, element detection, locator generation |
| **Test Service** | Provide screenshots, integrate detection with tests |
| **Recording Service** | Provide video frames for detection |
| **PostgreSQL** | Store detection results, elements, metadata |
| **Redis** | Cache detection results, model metadata |
| **S3/MinIO** | Store ML models, screenshots |

---

## 2. Detection Service Architecture

### 2.1 Service Components

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Detection Service (Python)                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

│  │ - Model management endpoints                         │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Business Logic Layer                                 │   │
│  │ - ObjectDetector                                     │   │
│  │ - ElementClassifier                                  │   │
│  │ - LocatorGenerator                                   │   │
│  │ - VisualRegression                                   │   │
│  │ - ModelManager                                       │   │

│  │ - YOLO Model                                         │   │
│  │ - Faster R-CNN Model                                 │   │

└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Key Classes/Interfaces

```python
# Object Detection
class ObjectDetector:
    def detect(self, image: np.ndarray, options: DetectionOptions) -> DetectionResult:
        """Detect UI elements in image"""
        pass
    
    def detect_batch(self, images: List[np.ndarray]) -> List[DetectionResult]:

        pass

class DetectionOptions:
    confidence_threshold: float = 0.7
    max_elements: int = 100
    element_types: List[str] = None
    region_of_interest: BoundingBox = None

class DetectionResult:
    detection_id: str
    elements: List[DetectedElement]
    detection_time_ms: int

class DetectedElement:
    element_id: str
    element_type: str
    bounding_box: BoundingBox

# Element Classification
class ElementClassifier:
    def classify(self, element: DetectedElement, image: np.ndarray) -> Classification:
        """Classify element type and state"""
        pass
    
    def extract_text(self, element: DetectedElement, image: np.ndarray) -> str:
        """Extract text using OCR"""
        pass
    
    def recognize_icon(self, element: DetectedElement, image: np.ndarray) -> str:
        """Recognize icon"""
        pass

class Classification:
    primary_type: str
    sub_type: str
    state: str
    attributes: List[str]
    confidence: float

# Locator Generation
class LocatorGenerator:
    def generate_locators(self, element: DetectedElement, 
                         screen_hierarchy: Dict) -> List[Locator]:
        """Generate multiple locator strategies"""
        pass
    
    def validate_locator(self, locator: Locator, 
                        screen_hierarchy: Dict) -> ValidationResult:
        """Validate locator uniqueness and stability"""
        pass

class Locator:
    strategy: str  # 'id', 'text', 'xpath', 'class', 'coordinates'
    value: str
    uniqueness_score: float
    stability_score: float

# Visual Regression
class VisualRegression:

               current: np.ndarray) -> RegressionResult:
        """Compare two screenshots for differences"""

    def detect_changes(self, baseline_elements: List[DetectedElement],
                      current_elements: List[DetectedElement]) -> List[Change]:
        """Detect element changes"""
        pass

class RegressionResult:
    differences: List[Difference]
    similarity_score: float
    changed_elements: List[str]
    new_elements: List[str]
    removed_elements: List[str]

class ModelManager:
```

## 3. Data Flow

### 3.1 Detection Flow

```
Screenshot received
    │
    ▼
ObjectDetector.detect()
    │
    ├─► Preprocess image (resize, normalize)
    │
    ├─► Run ML model inference
    │
    ├─► Post-process detections (NMS, filtering)
    │
    ├─► Extract bounding boxes and confidence scores
    │
    ▼
ElementClassifier.classify()
    │
    ├─► Classify element type
    │
    ├─► Extract text (OCR)
    │
    ├─► Recognize icon
    │
    ├─► Detect element state
    │
    ▼
LocatorGenerator.generate_locators()
    │
    ├─► Generate resource ID locator
    │
    ├─► Generate text locator
    │
    ├─► Generate XPath locator
    │
    ├─► Generate class locator
    │
    ├─► Validate locators
    │
    ▼
Store detection result in database
    │
    ├─► Cache in Redis (5-minute TTL)
    │
    ▼
Return detection result to client
```

### 3.2 Visual Regression Flow

```
Baseline and current screenshots received
    │
    ▼
Detect elements in both screenshots
    │
    ├─► Baseline elements
    │
    ├─► Current elements
    │
    ▼
VisualRegression.compare()
    │
    ├─► Match elements by locator
    │
    ├─► Compare positions
    │
    ├─► Compare sizes
    │
    ├─► Compare visual properties
    │
    ├─► Identify new elements
    │
    ├─► Identify removed elements
    │
    ▼
Generate regression report
    │
    ├─► List of changes
    │
    ├─► Visual diff image
    │
    ├─► Similarity score
    │
    ▼
Return regression result
```

## 4. Database Design

### 4.1 Schema Overview

```sql
-- Detection results
detection_results (
    id, screenshot_path, device_id, model_version,
    detection_time_ms, elements_detected, created_at
)

-- Detected elements
detected_elements (
    id, detection_result_id, element_type, bounding_box,
    confidence_score, properties, locators, created_at
)

-- Visual regression results
regression_results (
    id, baseline_screenshot, current_screenshot,
    similarity_score, changes_detected, created_at
)

-- Element changes
element_changes (
    id, regression_result_id, element_id, change_type,
    old_value, new_value, created_at
)
```

### 4.2 Indexing Strategy

```sql
-- For fast detection lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_detection_device_created 
ON detection_results(device_id, created_at DESC);

-- For element queries
CREATE INDEX idx_elements_detection_type 
ON detected_elements(detection_result_id, element_type);

-- For regression queries
CREATE INDEX idx_regression_created 
ON regression_results(created_at DESC);
```

## 7. ML Model Details

### 7.1 Model Architecture

```
Input: Screenshot (RGB image)
    │
    ▼
Preprocessing
    ├─► Resize to model input size (640x640 for YOLO)
    ├─► Normalize pixel values
    ├─► Convert to tensor
    │
    ▼
Backbone Network (Feature Extraction)
    ├─► Convolutional layers
    ├─► Extract features at multiple scales
    │
    ▼
Detection Head
    ├─► Predict bounding boxes
    ├─► Predict class probabilities
    ├─► Predict confidence scores
    │
    ▼
Post-processing
    ├─► Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS)
    ├─► Filter by confidence threshold
    ├─► Sort by confidence
    │
    ▼
Output: Detected elements with bounding boxes
```

### 7.2 Training Data

```
Training Dataset:
- 100,000+ Android UI screenshots
- Annotated with bounding boxes and labels
- Multiple Android versions (5.0 - 14.0)
- Various screen resolutions
- Different themes (light, dark, custom)
- Multiple apps (system, popular apps)

Element Classes:
- Button (10,000+ samples)
- Text Field (8,000+ samples)
- Label (15,000+ samples)
- Image (12,000+ samples)
- Checkbox (5,000+ samples)
- Switch (4,000+ samples)
- List Item (20,000+ samples)
- Icon (10,000+ samples)
- Dialog (3,000+ samples)
- Tab (4,000+ samples)
```

### 7.3 Model Performance

```
YOLO v8:
- mAP: 0.92
- Inference time: 50ms (GPU), 200ms (CPU)
- Model size: 50MB

Faster R-CNN:
- mAP: 0.95
- Inference time: 200ms (GPU), 800ms (CPU)
- Model size: 150MB

MobileNet:
- mAP: 0.88
- Inference time: 30ms (GPU), 100ms (CPU)
- Model size: 20MB
```

## 9. Performance Considerations

### 9.1 Inference Optimization
- Use GPU acceleration (CUDA)
- Batch processing for multiple images
- Model quantization for faster inference
- Cache detection results (5-minute TTL)

### 9.2 Scalability
- Horizontal scaling with multiple workers
- Load balancing across GPU instances
- Async processing for non-blocking operations
- Queue system for batch requests

### 9.3 Model Optimization
- Use TensorRT for optimized inference
- Prune unnecessary model layers
- Use mixed precision (FP16) for faster inference
- Edge deployment with lightweight models

---

## 10. Security Considerations

### 10.1 Authentication & Authorization
- Validate JWT tokens on all endpoints
- Check device access permissions
- Implement role-based access control
- Log all access attempts

### 10.2 Data Protection
- Encrypt screenshots at rest
- Use HTTPS/TLS for all communications
- Implement rate limiting
- Sanitize all user inputs

### 10.3 Model Security
- Verify model integrity (checksums)
- Secure model storage
- Prevent model tampering
- Audit model updates

---

## 11. Monitoring & Observability

### 11.1 Prometheus Metrics
```
detection_requests_total
detection_duration_seconds
detection_accuracy
model_inference_time_seconds
cache_hit_rate
gpu_utilization
```

### 11.2 Logging
- Log all detection requests
- Log model inference times
- Log errors and exceptions
- Log model updates

---

## 12. Error Handling

### 12.1 Detection Errors
- Invalid image: Return error with message
- Model not loaded: Load model and retry
- Inference timeout: Return partial results
- Low confidence: Return with warning

### 12.2 Model Errors
- Model load failed: Fallback to previous version
- Inference failed: Retry with different model
- Out of memory: Reduce batch size
- GPU unavailable: Fallback to CPU

### 13.1 Unit Tests
- Test image preprocessing
- Test model inference
- Test locator generation
- Test visual comparison
- Target: 80%+ coverage

### 13.2 Integration Tests
- Test end-to-end detection flow
- Test model switching
- Test caching
- Test API endpoints

### 13.3 Model Evaluation
- Evaluate on test dataset
- Measure mAP, precision, recall

# Design Document: Remote File Manager

**Feature ID:** RFM-001  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-17  
**Status:** Draft

## 1. Architecture Overview

### 1.1 System Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Desktop App (Tauri)                       │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  React UI Components                                     │   │
│  │  - File Browser                                          │   │
│  │  - Upload/Download Manager                               │   │
│  │  - Multi-device View                                     │   │
│  │  - Storage Manager                                       │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ▲
                              │ HTTPS/WebSocket
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      API Gateway                                 │
│  - Authentication (JWT validation)                              │
│  - Rate limiting                                                │
│  - Request routing                                              │
│  - Streaming support                                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ▲
        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
        ▼                     ▼                     ▼
┌────────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐
│ Device         │    │ Test           │    │ File           │
│ Service        │    │ Service        │    │ Service        │
│ (gRPC)         │    │ (gRPC)         │    │ (gRPC)         │
└────────────────┘    └────────────────┘    └────────────────┘
        ▲                     ▲                     ▲
        └─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘
                              ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  PostgreSQL + Redis + Local Storage     │
        │  - File operations                      │
        │  - Transfer queue                       │
        │  - Temporary files                      │
        └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.2 Component Responsibilities

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|-----------------|
| **Desktop App** | Display file browser, upload/download UI, multi-device view |
| **API Gateway** | Route requests, validate auth, rate limiting, streaming |
| **File Service** | Manage file operations, transfers, storage |
| **Device Service** | Provide device connection and ADB access |
| **Test Service** | Trigger file operations for test data |
| **PostgreSQL** | Store file operations, transfer history |
| **Redis** | Queue file operations, cache file metadata |
| **Local Storage** | Temporary storage for file transfers |

## 2. File Service Architecture

### 2.1 Service Components

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              File Service (Go)                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ API Layer (gRPC + REST)                              │   │
│  │ - File browser endpoints                             │   │
│  │ - Upload/download endpoints                          │   │
│  │ - File operation endpoints                           │   │
│  │ - Storage management endpoints                       │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Business Logic Layer                                 │   │
│  │ - FileBrowser                                        │   │
│  │ - FileTransferManager                                │   │
│  │ - FileOperationManager                               │   │
│  │ - StorageManager                                     │   │
│  │ - SyncManager                                        │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Data Access Layer                                    │   │
│  │ - OperationRepository                                │   │
│  │ - TransferRepository                                 │   │
│  │ - FileMetadataCache                                  │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                          ▲                                   │
│                          │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Infrastructure Layer                                 │   │
│  │ - PostgreSQL driver                                  │   │
│  │ - Redis client                                       │   │
│  │ - ADB client                                         │   │
│  │ - Device service client                              │   │
│  │ - Local file system                                  │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Key Classes/Interfaces

```go
// File Browsing
type FileBrowser interface {
    ListFiles(ctx context.Context, deviceID, path string, filter *FileFilter) (*FileList, error)
    GetFileInfo(ctx context.Context, deviceID, path string) (*FileInfo, error)
    SearchFiles(ctx context.Context, deviceID string, query *SearchQuery) ([]*FileInfo, error)
    GetStorageInfo(ctx context.Context, deviceID string) (*StorageInfo, error)
}

type FileInfo struct {
    Name         string
    Path         string
    Size         int64
    Type         string // "file", "directory"
    Extension    string
    Permissions  string
    ModifiedAt   time.Time
    IsHidden     bool
}

type FileList struct {
    Files       []*FileInfo
    TotalCount  int32
    CurrentPath string
}

type SearchQuery struct {
    Query       string
    FileType    string
    SizeMin     int64
    SizeMax     int64
    DateFrom    *time.Time
    DateTo      *time.Time
    Recursive   bool
}

// File Transfer
type FileTransferManager interface {
    UploadFile(ctx context.Context, req *UploadRequest) (*Transfer, error)
    DownloadFile(ctx context.Context, req *DownloadRequest) (*Transfer, error)
    GetTransferStatus(ctx context.Context, transferID string) (*Transfer, error)
    CancelTransfer(ctx context.Context, transferID string) error
    ResumeTransfer(ctx context.Context, transferID string) error
}

type UploadRequest struct {
    DeviceID        string
    SourcePath      string // local file path
    DestinationPath string // device path
    Overwrite       bool
    Checksum        string
}

type DownloadRequest struct {
    DeviceID        string
    SourcePath      string // device path
    DestinationPath string // local file path
    Checksum        string
}

type Transfer struct {
    ID              string
    DeviceID        string
    Type            string // "upload", "download"
    SourcePath      string
    DestinationPath string
    FileSize        int64
    BytesTransferred int64
    Progress        int32 // 0-100
    Speed           int64 // bytes per second
    Status          string // "pending", "in_progress", "completed", "failed", "cancelled"
    ErrorMessage    string
    StartedAt       time.Time
    CompletedAt     *time.Time
}

// File Operations
type FileOperationManager interface {
    CopyFile(ctx context.Context, req *CopyRequest) (*Operation, error)
    MoveFile(ctx context.Context, req *MoveRequest) (*Operation, error)
    DeleteFile(ctx context.Context, req *DeleteRequest) (*Operation, error)
    RenameFile(ctx context.Context, req *RenameRequest) (*Operation, error)
    CreateDirectory(ctx context.Context, deviceID, path string) error
    CompressFiles(ctx context.Context, req *CompressRequest) (*Operation, error)
    ExtractArchive(ctx context.Context, req *ExtractRequest) (*Operation, error)
}

type CopyRequest struct {
    DeviceID    string
    SourcePath  string
    DestPath    string
    Overwrite   bool
}

type DeleteRequest struct {
    DeviceID    string
    Paths       []string
    Permanent   bool
}

type Operation struct {
    ID          string
    DeviceID    string
    Type        string // "copy", "move", "delete", "compress", "extract"
    Status      string // "pending", "in_progress", "completed", "failed"
    Progress    int32
    ErrorMessage string
    CreatedAt   time.Time
    CompletedAt *time.Time
}

// Storage Management
type StorageManager interface {
    GetStorageInfo(ctx context.Context, deviceID string) (*StorageInfo, error)
    FindDuplicates(ctx context.Context, deviceID, path string) ([]*DuplicateGroup, error)
    FindLargeFiles(ctx context.Context, deviceID string, minSize int64) ([]*FileInfo, error)
    CleanupStorage(ctx context.Context, req *CleanupRequest) (*CleanupResult, error)
}

type StorageInfo struct {
    TotalSpace     int64
    UsedSpace      int64
    AvailableSpace int64
    Breakdown      map[string]int64 // type -> size
}

type DuplicateGroup struct {
    Checksum string
    Files    []*FileInfo
    TotalSize int64
}

type CleanupRequest struct {
    DeviceID       string
    DeleteDuplicates bool
    DeleteOldFiles   bool
    OlderThanDays    int32
    ClearCache       bool
}

// Sync Management
type SyncManager interface {
    SyncFiles(ctx context.Context, req *SyncRequest) (*SyncResult, error)
    GetSyncStatus(ctx context.Context, syncID string) (*SyncStatus, error)
}

type SyncRequest struct {
    SourceDeviceID  string
    TargetDeviceIDs []string
    Paths           []string
    Mode            string // "mirror", "incremental"
}

type SyncResult struct {
    SyncID       string
    SuccessCount int32
    FailureCount int32
    Results      map[string]*SyncDeviceResult // device_id -> result
}
```

---

## 3. Data Flow

### 3.1 File Upload Flow

```
User selects file to upload
    │
    ▼
FileTransferManager.UploadFile()
    │
    ├─► Create Transfer record in DB
    │
    ├─► Check device storage space
    │
    ├─► Read file from local filesystem
    │
    ├─► Split into chunks (1MB each)
    │
    ├─► For each chunk:
    │   ├─► Transfer via ADB push
    │   ├─► Update progress
    │   ├─► Broadcast progress via WebSocket
    │
    ├─► Verify file integrity (checksum)
    │
    ├─► Update Transfer status to "completed"
    │
    ▼
Notify user of completion
```

### 3.2 File Download Flow

```
User selects file to download
    │
    ▼
FileTransferManager.DownloadFile()
    │
    ├─► Create Transfer record in DB
    │
    ├─► Check local disk space
    │
    ├─► Pull file from device via ADB
    │
    ├─► Split into chunks (1MB each)
    │
    ├─► For each chunk:
    │   ├─► Transfer via ADB pull
    │   ├─► Write to local filesystem
    │   ├─► Update progress
    │   ├─► Broadcast progress via WebSocket
    │
    ├─► Verify file integrity (checksum)
    │
    ├─► Update Transfer status to "completed"
    │
    ▼
Notify user of completion
```

### 3.3 File Sync Flow

```
User initiates sync
    │
    ▼
SyncManager.SyncFiles()
    │
    ├─► Get file list from source device
    │
    ├─► For each target device:
    │   ├─► Get file list from target device
    │   ├─► Compare file lists
    │   ├─► Identify files to sync
    │   ├─► Transfer files
    │   ├─► Verify integrity
    │   ├─► Update sync status
    │
    ├─► Aggregate results
    │
    ▼
Return sync result
```

---

## 4. Database Design

### 4.1 Schema Overview

```sql
-- File operations
file_operations (
    id, device_id, operation_type, source_path,
    destination_path, file_size, status, progress,
    error_message, created_by, created_at, completed_at
)

-- File transfers
file_transfers (
    id, operation_id, chunk_number, chunk_size,
    checksum, transferred_at
)

-- Sync operations
sync_operations (
    id, source_device_id, target_device_ids,
    paths, mode, status, created_at, completed_at
)

-- Sync results
sync_results (
    id, sync_operation_id, device_id, status,
    files_synced, bytes_transferred, error_message
)
```

### 4.2 Indexing Strategy

```sql
-- For fast operation lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_operations_device_created 
ON file_operations(device_id, created_at DESC);

-- For transfer queries
CREATE INDEX idx_transfers_operation 
ON file_transfers(operation_id, chunk_number);

-- For sync queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sync_created 
ON sync_operations(created_at DESC);
```

## 5. API Design

### 5.1 REST Endpoints

```
GET /api/v1/files/browse
  - Query params: device_id, path, filter
  - Returns: File list

POST /api/v1/files/upload
  - Body: multipart/form-data
  - Returns: { transfer_id, status }

GET /api/v1/files/download
  - Query params: device_id, path
  - Returns: File stream

POST /api/v1/files/delete
  - Body: { device_id, paths[], permanent }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

POST /api/v1/files/copy
  - Body: { device_id, source, destination }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

POST /api/v1/files/move
  - Body: { device_id, source, destination }
  - Returns: { operation_id, status }

POST /api/v1/files/rename
  - Body: { device_id, old_name, new_name }
  - Returns: { success: true }

GET /api/v1/files/search
  - Query params: device_id, query, filters
  - Returns: Search results

GET /api/v1/files/storage
  - Query params: device_id
  - Returns: Storage information

POST /api/v1/files/sync
  - Body: { source_device_id, target_device_ids[], paths[] }
  - Returns: { sync_id, status }

GET /api/v1/files/operations/{operation_id}
  - Returns: Operation status and progress

GET /api/v1/files/transfers/{transfer_id}
  - Returns: Transfer status and progress
```

### 5.2 WebSocket API

```
WS /ws/files/transfers/{transfer_id}
  - Subscribe to transfer progress updates
  - Message: { type: "progress", progress, speed, eta }
  - Message: { type: "completed", checksum }
  - Message: { type: "failed", error }
```

- **Containerization:** Docker
- **Orchestration:** Kubernetes (optional)
- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions or GitLab CI

---

## 7. File Transfer Optimization

### 7.1 ADB Commands

```bash
# List files
adb shell ls -la /sdcard/

# Upload file
adb push local_file.txt /sdcard/remote_file.txt

# Download file
adb pull /sdcard/remote_file.txt local_file.txt

# Delete file
adb shell rm /sdcard/file.txt

# Create directory
adb shell mkdir /sdcard/new_folder

# Get storage info
adb shell df /sdcard/

# Get file checksum
adb shell md5sum /sdcard/file.txt
```

### 7.2 Transfer Optimization

```
Chunked Transfer:
- Split large files into 1MB chunks
- Transfer chunks in parallel (up to 5 concurrent)
- Resume from last successful chunk on failure

Compression:
- Compress files before transfer (optional)
- Use gzip or zstd compression
- Decompress on destination

Checksum Verification:
- Calculate MD5/SHA256 before transfer
- Verify after transfer
- Retry on mismatch
```

---

## 8. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Core File Browsing (Week 1)
- [ ] Set up file service project
- [ ] Implement file browser
- [ ] Implement file search
- [ ] Create database schema
- [ ] Implement basic REST API

### Phase 2: File Upload/Download (Week 1-2)
- [ ] Implement upload manager
- [ ] Implement download manager
- [ ] Implement chunked transfer
- [ ] Add progress tracking
- [ ] Add WebSocket streaming

### Phase 3: File Operations (Week 2)
- [ ] Implement copy/move/delete
- [ ] Implement rename
- [ ] Implement compress/extract
- [ ] Add batch operations
- [ ] Add error handling

### Phase 4: Storage Management (Week 2-3)
- [ ] Implement storage info
- [ ] Implement duplicate finder
- [ ] Implement cleanup tools
- [ ] Add storage visualization

### Phase 5: Multi-device & Sync (Week 3)
- [ ] Implement multi-device view
- [ ] Implement file sync
- [ ] Implement bulk operations
- [ ] Add conflict resolution

### Phase 6: Testing & Deployment (Week 3-4)
- [ ] End-to-end testing
- [ ] Performance testing
- [ ] Security testing
- [ ] Deployment

---

## 9. Performance Considerations

### 9.1 Transfer Performance
- Use parallel chunk transfers
- Implement connection pooling
- Optimize ADB commands
- Cache file metadata

### 9.2 Scalability
- Support 50+ concurrent transfers
- Efficient database queries
- Queue system for operations
- Load balancing

### 9.3 Storage Optimization
- Compress files before transfer
- Clean up temporary files
- Implement file deduplication
- Use efficient storage formats

---

## 10. Security Considerations

### 10.1 Authentication & Authorization
- Validate JWT tokens on all endpoints
- Check device access permissions
- Implement role-based access control
- Log all file operations

### 10.2 Data Protection
- Encrypt file transfers (HTTPS/TLS)
- Validate file types
- Prevent path traversal attacks
- Sanitize file names

### 10.3 File Access Control
- Respect device permissions
- Prevent access to system files (without root)
- Validate file paths
- Audit file access

---

## 11. Monitoring & Observability

### 11.1 Prometheus Metrics
```
file_operations_total{type, status}
file_transfer_duration_seconds
file_transfer_speed_mbps
file_operations_failed_total
storage_usage_bytes{device_id}
```

### 11.2 Logging
- Log all file operations
- Log transfer progress
- Log errors and failures
- Log storage usage

---

## 12. Error Handling

### 12.1 Transfer Errors
- Device disconnected: Pause transfer, attempt resume
- Insufficient storage: Abort with error
- Permission denied: Log and notify user
- Network error: Retry with exponential backoff

### 12.2 Operation Errors
- File not found: Return 404
- Permission denied: Return 403
- Operation failed: Retry or abort
- Timeout: Cancel operation

---

## 13. Testing Strategy

### 13.1 Unit Tests
- Test file browser
- Test file transfer
- Test file operations
- Test storage management
- Target: 80%+ coverage

### 13.2 Integration Tests
- Test end-to-end upload/download
- Test file sync
- Test bulk operations
- Test error scenarios

### 13.3 Performance Tests
- Load test with 50+ concurrent transfers
- Stress test large file transfers (2GB)
- Test transfer speed (> 10 MB/s)
- Measure latency

---

## 14. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-05-17 | Engineering Team | Initial design document |

**Priority:** Medium  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-17  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
Network traffic monitoring system that captures, analyzes, and displays HTTP/HTTPS network requests and responses from Android devices, enabling debugging, performance analysis, and API testing with request/response inspection, filtering, and export capabilities.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Enable network debugging for mobile applications
- Support API testing and validation
- Identify performance bottlenecks in network calls
- Monitor bandwidth usage and data consumption
- Detect security issues (unencrypted traffic, API leaks)
- Reduce debugging time by 60%

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- Capture 100% of HTTP/HTTPS traffic
- Real-time traffic display latency < 500ms
- Support 50+ concurrent monitoring sessions
- Traffic analysis completion < 2 seconds
- Export traffic logs within 5 seconds

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 Traffic Capture

#### 2.1.1 Capture Methods
**REQ-NTM-001:** The system SHALL capture network traffic using:
- **ADB tcpdump:** Capture raw packets via ADB
- **Proxy method:** Route traffic through proxy server
- **VPN method:** Capture via VPN service on device
- **Root method:** Direct packet capture (requires root)

**REQ-NTM-002:** Traffic capture SHALL support:
- HTTP requests and responses
- HTTPS requests and responses (with SSL decryption)
- WebSocket connections
- DNS queries
- TCP/UDP packets (optional)

**REQ-NTM-003:** The system SHALL capture the following information:
- Request URL and method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)
- Request headers
- Request body (JSON, XML, form data, binary)
- Response status code
- Response headers
- Response body
- Request/response timestamps
- Request/response size
- Duration (time to first byte, total time)

**REQ-NTM-004:** Capture SHALL work for:
- All apps on device
- Specific app (filter by package name)
- System traffic
- Background traffic

#### 2.1.2 SSL/TLS Decryption
**REQ-NTM-005:** The system SHALL support HTTPS traffic decryption:
- Install CA certificate on device
- Man-in-the-middle proxy for SSL decryption
- Certificate pinning bypass (with user consent)
- Display encrypted traffic metadata (without decryption)

**REQ-NTM-006:** SSL decryption SHALL:
- Require user confirmation
- Display security warning
- Support certificate trust management
- Handle certificate pinning gracefully

**REQ-NTM-007:** For apps with certificate pinning:
- Display encrypted traffic metadata only
- Show certificate information
- Indicate pinning is active
- Provide bypass instructions (if possible)

#### 2.1.3 Capture Control
**REQ-NTM-008:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Start traffic capture
- Stop traffic capture
- Pause capture (buffer traffic)
- Resume capture
- Clear captured traffic

**REQ-NTM-009:** Capture settings SHALL be configurable:
- Capture method (ADB, proxy, VPN)
- SSL decryption (on/off)
- Capture filter (all apps, specific app)
- Buffer size (default 10MB, max 100MB)
- Auto-save interval (default 5 minutes)

**REQ-NTM-010:** The system SHALL handle capture limits:
- Maximum buffer size (100MB)
- Maximum capture duration (60 minutes)
- Automatic rotation when limit reached
- Warning when approaching limits

### 2.2 Traffic Display

#### 2.2.1 Traffic List View
**REQ-NTM-011:** The system SHALL display captured traffic in list view:
- Request method and URL
- Status code
- Response time
- Request/response size
- Timestamp
- App name/package

**REQ-NTM-012:** Traffic list SHALL support:
- Real-time updates (as traffic is captured)
- Sorting (by time, size, duration, status)
- Filtering (by method, status, app, URL pattern)
- Search (by URL, header, body content)
- Color coding (by status code, method)

**REQ-NTM-013:** Traffic list SHALL display:
- Success requests (2xx) in green
- Redirects (3xx) in blue
- Client errors (4xx) in orange
- Server errors (5xx) in red
- Pending requests in gray

#### 2.2.2 Request/Response Details
**REQ-NTM-014:** The system SHALL display detailed request information:
- Full URL with query parameters
- HTTP method
- Request headers (formatted)
- Request body (formatted JSON/XML, raw, hex)
- Request size
- Timestamp

**REQ-NTM-015:** The system SHALL display detailed response information:
- Status code and message
- Response headers (formatted)
- Response body (formatted JSON/XML, raw, hex, image preview)
- Response size
- Response time breakdown (DNS, connect, TLS, TTFB, download)

**REQ-NTM-016:** Request/response display SHALL support:
- Syntax highlighting (JSON, XML, HTML)
- Pretty printing
- Search within content
- Copy to clipboard
- Save to file

#### 2.2.3 Timeline View
**REQ-NTM-017:** The system SHALL provide timeline view:
- Waterfall chart of requests
- Request duration visualization
- Concurrent requests display
- Time markers
- Zoom and pan

**REQ-NTM-018:** Timeline SHALL show:
- DNS lookup time
- TCP connection time
- SSL handshake time
- Time to first byte (TTFB)
- Content download time
- Total request time

### 2.3 Traffic Filtering

#### 2.3.1 Filter Options
**REQ-NTM-019:** Users SHALL be able to filter traffic by:
- HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)
- Status code (2xx, 3xx, 4xx, 5xx)
- Content type (JSON, XML, HTML, image, etc.)
- App/package name
- URL pattern (regex, wildcard)
- Request/response size (range)
- Duration (range)
- Time range

**REQ-NTM-020:** The system SHALL provide quick filters:
- Errors only (4xx, 5xx)
- Images only
- API calls only (JSON/XML)
- Slow requests (> 1 second)
- Large requests (> 1MB)

**REQ-NTM-021:** Filters SHALL be:
- Combinable (multiple filters active)
- Saveable as presets
- Shareable with other users
- Applied in real-time

#### 2.3.2 Search Functionality
**REQ-NTM-022:** The system SHALL support search:
- Search in URL
- Search in request headers
- Search in request body
- Search in response headers
- Search in response body
- Regular expression search

**REQ-NTM-023:** Search SHALL:
- Highlight matching text
- Show match count
- Navigate between matches
- Support case-sensitive/insensitive
- Support whole word matching

### 2.4 Traffic Analysis

#### 2.4.1 Performance Analysis
**REQ-NTM-024:** The system SHALL provide performance analysis:
- Average response time per endpoint
- Slowest requests
- Largest requests/responses
- Request frequency per endpoint
- Error rate per endpoint
- Bandwidth usage over time

**REQ-NTM-025:** Performance metrics SHALL include:
- Total requests count
- Success rate (2xx responses)
- Error rate (4xx, 5xx responses)
- Average response time
- P50, P95, P99 response times
- Total data transferred (upload/download)

**REQ-NTM-026:** The system SHALL detect performance issues:
- Slow API calls (> 3 seconds)
- Large payloads (> 1MB)
- Excessive requests (> 100/minute)
- Failed requests (> 10% error rate)
- Duplicate requests

#### 2.4.2 API Analysis
**REQ-NTM-027:** The system SHALL analyze API usage:
- Unique endpoints called
- Request/response patterns
- API versioning
- Authentication methods used
- Common headers
- Common parameters

**REQ-NTM-028:** API analysis SHALL identify:
- REST API endpoints
- GraphQL queries
- WebSocket connections
- Third-party API calls
- Analytics/tracking calls

#### 2.4.3 Security Analysis
**REQ-NTM-029:** The system SHALL detect security issues:
- Unencrypted HTTP traffic (should be HTTPS)
- Sensitive data in URLs (passwords, tokens)
- Weak authentication (Basic Auth over HTTP)
- API keys in requests
- Insecure cookies (no Secure flag)
- Missing security headers

**REQ-NTM-030:** Security warnings SHALL:
- Highlight in red
- Provide severity level (critical, warning, info)
- Suggest fixes
- Link to security best practices

### 2.5 Traffic Export

#### 2.5.1 Export Formats
**REQ-NTM-031:** The system SHALL support export in formats:
- HAR (HTTP Archive) - standard format
- JSON - structured data
- CSV - tabular data
- cURL commands - for replay
- Postman collection - for API testing
- Charles session - for Charles Proxy

**REQ-NTM-032:** Export SHALL include:
- All captured traffic or filtered subset
- Request/response headers and bodies
- Timing information
- Metadata (device, app, timestamp)

**REQ-NTM-033:** Export options SHALL allow:
- Select specific requests to export
- Export all traffic
- Export filtered traffic
- Export with/without response bodies
- Compress export (ZIP)

#### 2.5.2 Export Destinations
**REQ-NTM-034:** Users SHALL be able to export to:
- Local file (download)
- Cloud storage (future)
- Share via link (future)
- Send to external tools (Postman, Insomnia)

### 2.6 Traffic Replay

#### 2.6.1 Request Replay
**REQ-NTM-035:** Users SHALL be able to replay requests:
- Replay single request
- Replay multiple requests
- Modify request before replay
- Replay with different parameters

**REQ-NTM-036:** Request modification SHALL support:
- Edit URL
- Edit headers
- Edit body
- Change method
- Add/remove parameters

**REQ-NTM-037:** Replay SHALL:
- Execute from desktop (not device)
- Display new response
- Compare with original response
- Save replay results

#### 2.6.2 Mock Responses
**REQ-NTM-038:** The system SHALL support response mocking:
- Intercept specific requests
- Return mock response
- Modify response before returning
- Simulate delays
- Simulate errors

**REQ-NTM-039:** Mock configuration SHALL allow:
- Match by URL pattern
- Match by method
- Match by headers
- Define mock response body
- Define mock status code
- Define mock headers

### 2.7 Integration with Test Automation

#### 2.7.1 Test Integration
**REQ-NTM-040:** The system SHALL integrate with test automation:
- Start capture before test execution
- Stop capture after test execution
- Associate traffic with test results
- Validate API responses in tests
- Assert on network calls

**REQ-NTM-041:** Test assertions SHALL support:
- Assert request was made
- Assert response status code
- Assert response body contains
- Assert response time < threshold
- Assert no errors occurred

#### 2.7.2 API Validation
**REQ-NTM-042:** The system SHALL support API validation:
- Validate response schema (JSON Schema)
- Validate response structure
- Validate response data types
- Validate required fields
- Compare with API specification (OpenAPI)

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-NTM-NFR-001:** Traffic capture SHALL NOT impact device performance by more than 5% CPU.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-002:** Real-time traffic display latency SHALL be < 500ms.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-003:** The system SHALL support 50+ concurrent monitoring sessions.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-004:** Traffic analysis SHALL complete within 2 seconds for 1000+ requests.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-005:** Export SHALL complete within 5 seconds for 10,000+ requests.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-NTM-NFR-006:** The system SHALL scale to support 1000+ devices.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-007:** Traffic buffer SHALL support up to 100MB per session.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-008:** Database SHALL efficiently store 1M+ captured requests.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-NTM-NFR-009:** Traffic capture reliability SHALL be > 99%.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-010:** No traffic SHALL be lost during capture.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-011:** Capture SHALL continue after device reconnection.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-012:** Captured traffic SHALL be persisted every 5 minutes.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-NTM-NFR-013:** All traffic monitoring APIs SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-014:** Captured traffic SHALL be encrypted at rest.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-015:** SSL decryption SHALL require explicit user consent.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-016:** Access to captured traffic SHALL be logged for audit.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-NTM-NFR-017:** Traffic service SHALL be implemented as separate microservice in Go.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-018:** Codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-019:** System SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-NTM-NFR-020:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Device Service Integration
**REQ-NTM-INT-001:** Traffic service SHALL integrate with device-service for device management.

**REQ-NTM-INT-002:** Device-service SHALL provide device connection status.

### 4.2 Test Service Integration
**REQ-NTM-INT-003:** Traffic service SHALL integrate with test-service for test validation.

**REQ-NTM-INT-004:** Test-service SHALL trigger traffic capture via API.

### 4.3 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-NTM-INT-005:** All traffic APIs SHALL be exposed through api-gateway.

**REQ-NTM-INT-006:** API-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting for traffic endpoints.

### 4.4 Storage Integration
**REQ-NTM-INT-007:** Traffic service SHALL store captured traffic in PostgreSQL.

**REQ-NTM-INT-008:** Traffic service SHALL cache recent traffic in Redis.

**REQ-NTM-INT-009:** Traffic service SHALL store large payloads in S3/MinIO.

---

## 5. User Interface Requirements

### 5.1 Traffic Monitor UI
**REQ-NTM-UI-001:** Traffic monitor interface SHALL display:
- Traffic list with real-time updates
- Request/response details panel
- Timeline view
- Filter controls
- Capture controls

### 5.2 Analysis UI
**REQ-NTM-UI-002:** Analysis interface SHALL display:
- Performance metrics
- API usage statistics
- Security warnings
- Charts and graphs

### 5.3 Export UI
**REQ-NTM-UI-003:** Export interface SHALL display:
- Format selection
- Export options
- Progress indicator
- Download link

---

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 traffic_sessions Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE traffic_sessions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    app_package VARCHAR(255),
    capture_method VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    ssl_decryption BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    ended_at TIMESTAMP,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id)
);
```

#### 6.1.2 captured_requests Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE captured_requests (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    session_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES traffic_sessions(id),
    method VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    request_headers JSONB,
    request_body TEXT,
    response_status INTEGER,
    response_headers JSONB,
    response_body TEXT,
    duration_ms INTEGER,
    request_size BIGINT,
    response_size BIGINT,
    timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);
```

---

## 7. API Requirements

### 7.1 REST API Endpoints

```
POST /api/v1/traffic/sessions/start
  - Body: { device_id, app_package, capture_method, ssl_decryption }
  - Returns: { session_id, status }

POST /api/v1/traffic/sessions/{session_id}/stop
  - Returns: { session_id, status }

GET /api/v1/traffic/sessions/{session_id}/requests
  - Query params: filter, limit, offset
  - Returns: Request list

GET /api/v1/traffic/requests/{request_id}
  - Returns: Request details

POST /api/v1/traffic/requests/{request_id}/replay
  - Body: { modifications }
  - Returns: { response }

POST /api/v1/traffic/sessions/{session_id}/export
  - Body: { format, options }
  - Returns: { export_id, download_url }

GET /api/v1/traffic/sessions/{session_id}/analysis
  - Returns: Analysis results
```

---

## 8. Acceptance Criteria

### 8.1 Feature Complete Criteria
- [ ] All functional requirements implemented
- [ ] All non-functional requirements met
- [ ] UI components implemented
- [ ] Database schema deployed
- [ ] API documentation completed
- [ ] Unit tests achieve 80%+ coverage
- [ ] Integration tests cover critical flows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met

### 8.2 User Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can capture HTTP/HTTPS traffic
- [ ] Traffic displayed in real-time
- [ ] SSL decryption working
- [ ] Filtering and search working
- [ ] Performance analysis working
- [ ] Export working in multiple formats
- [ ] Integration with test automation working

---

## 9. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-05-17 | Engineering Team | Initial requirements document |
